value.Title: Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-95G3-SFDD-V19G-00000-00Skyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:02:37ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures, HiveTracks expand partnership to multi-state solar portfolio

Renewable energy investor Skyview Ventures and its solar firm Davis Hill Development has expanded its partnership with HiveTracks to integrate apiaries and environmental monitoring across its solar portfolio.

The companys current solar offerings span multiple states, including Maryland, Connecticut, and New York, plus Washington, D.C. Using HiveTracks biodiversity intelligence platform will allow the company to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across several solar projects, incorporating pollinator habitats into existing solar projects.

This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind, says Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems.

Skyview and HiveTracks have been working together since 2025, the companies say, beginning their collaboration with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Thanks to the partnerships initial success, the two firms have planned expansion to additional Davis Hill sites throughout 2026.

Local beekeepers will tend to each solar site throughout the lifespan of its given apiary, the company says. Beyond the production of bottles of honey which in 2025 was given away to food banks the apiaries will serve as environmental indicators, giving the companies numbers on ecosystem health and land use impact over time.

solar apiary

Apiary analysis

As part of the agreement, HiveTrack will collect environmental metrics for the solar sites, both on a per-site and portfolio-wide level, company officials say. The metrics will give insight to pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, as well as testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology in each hive.

Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further, says Maria Morales Ferrebus, solar development associate at Skyview Ventures. In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk.

By implementing HiveTracks recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats.

The apiary program currently spans multiple projects throughout varying stages of development, the company says, with some affected projects already fully operational. This approach allows Skyview to monitor environmental impacts of solar projects through each stage of development, representatives say, with HiveTracks providing site-specific recommendations.

The heart of the program reflects a wider trend around the solar industry, Skyview says, where developers now have an outsized focus on multi-use land strategies. As part of the shift, solar projects have become synonymous with farming, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives around the U.S. and beyond.

Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale, says Max Rnzel, CEO of HiveTracks. Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact.

value.Title: Supporting Energy and Economic Development (SEED) Act Legislation by Sen. Blackburn Analyzed

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-H013-RVB7-J3FM-00000-00Supporting Energy and Economic Development (SEED) Act Legislation by Sen. Blackburn Analyzed2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisSupporting Energy and Economic Development (SEED) Act Legislation by Sen. Blackburn AnalyzedBailey Malota, Targeted News Service

WASHINGTON, May 11 -- The Supporting Energy and Economic Development (SEED) Act, originally introduced by Sen. Marsha Blackburn, R-TN, on April 28, 2026, has been analyzed by the Congressional Research Service. The legislation aims to extend existing incentives for biodiesel and renewable diesel, promoting the production and use of cleaner fuels.

The SEED Act seeks to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 by extending the biodiesel mixture tax credit and excise tax incentives through 2029. Currently, these incentives are set to expire at the end of 2024, and the legislation addresses this gap, aiming to foster growth in the biodiesel industry and reduce dependence on fossil fuels. By providing financial benefits to biodiesel producers, the bill is designed to enhance energy independence and economic development, particularly in rural communities where biodiesel production often flourishes.

The motivation behind the SEED Act aligns with broader national efforts to transition to renewable energy sources as concerns about climate change grow. Leveraging biodiesel presents an opportunity to utilize domestic agricultural products, thereby supporting local economies and job creation. The bill also includes provisions to prevent the double-dipping of benefits, ensuring that producers maximize their incentives without taking undue advantage of the tax code.

Should the SEED Act pass through Congress, it will mark a significant step toward solidifying the foundations of renewable energy policy in the United States. With projections showing increasing demand for sustainable energy solutions, supporters argue that extending these biodiesel incentives is crucial for stimulating innovation within the sector while addressing environmental concerns. Ultimately, the SEED Act represents an ambitious push toward a greener, more sustainable energy future.

The bill (S. 4408) has no co-sponsors.

* * # * *

value.Title: Energy Tax Reform Legislation by Rep. Fitzpatrick Analyzed

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-T4S3-RX1F-W0WY-00000-00Energy Tax Reform Legislation by Rep. Fitzpatrick Analyzed2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisEnergy Tax Reform Legislation by Rep. Fitzpatrick AnalyzedBailey Malota, Targeted News Service

WASHINGTON, May 1 -- The Energy Tax Reform, originally introduced by Rep. Brian K. Fitzpatrick, R-PA, on April 23, 2026, has been analyzed by the Congressional Research Service. This legislation aims to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to reverse specific energy-related modifications enacted by Public Law 119-21, significantly altering incentives for energy efficiency and clean energy production.

The bill's primary focus is the repeal of several key provisions that previously promoted energy-efficient commercial buildings and residential homes. Notably, the Energy Efficient Commercial Buildings Deduction and the New Energy Efficient Home Credit would see their expiration dates adjusted or eliminated, while credits for clean hydrogen production and electric energy production would also be modified to extend deadlines or alter the conditions for their availability. The revisions intend to simplify tax provisions while potentially recalibrating the landscape for energy investments.

The motivation behind this legislation stems from ongoing discussions regarding the effectiveness of current fiscal incentives in driving sustainable energy practices. The current administration, which previously championed the provisions of Public Law 119-21 aimed at boosting renewable energy and reducing emissions, may view these reversals as a step back from aggressive climate goals. Critics of Rep. Fitzpatrick's bill worry it may diminish support for clean energy initiatives during a critical transition period for the climate.

The potential impact of this legislation is significant, affecting both businesses and homeowners seeking to leverage tax credits for energy efficiency improvements. As discussions continue in the Committee on Ways and Means, stakeholders from various sectors are closely monitoring how these changes could reshape the energy market in the United States as it grapples with competing priorities between economic growth and environmental stewardship.

The bill (H.R. 8477) has 3 co-sponsors: Reps. Michael Lawler, R-NY; Max L. Miller, R-OH; Mike Carey, R-OH.

* * # * *

Primary source of information: https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/8477/text

value.Title: May 08, 2026: NTU Honors Zhao Yanli with AIMBE Fellowship for Breakthroughs in Targeted Imaging and Therapy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-7YM3-RS4R-20WF-00000-00May 08, 2026: NTU Honors Zhao Yanli with AIMBE Fellowship for Breakthroughs in Targeted Imaging and Therapy2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 08, 2026: NTU Honors Zhao Yanli with AIMBE Fellowship for Breakthroughs in Targeted Imaging and Therapy

Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) announced that Professor Zhao Yanli from the School of Chemistry, Chemical Engineering and Biotechnology has been elected as a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering (AIMBE). His induction occurred on April 13, 2026, at the AIMBE Annual Event in Arlington, Virginia, USA.

The distinction recognizes Zhao’s pioneering interdisciplinary research in materials chemistry and biomedical engineering, with contributions spanning bioimaging, therapy and nanomedicine platforms. He is ranked among the top two percent of medical and biological engineers, and the AIMBE College of Fellows is among the sector’s highest professional honors.

At NTU Zhao leads a group that builds integrated functional systems for targeted bioimaging and disease therapy. The team has advanced therapeutic platforms that enable optical imaging while delivering drugs precisely where needed, including work on single-metal atom biocatalysts for antitumor immunotherapy.

These biocatalysts combine high activity and selectivity with well defined structures and are designed to suit the microenvironments of different diseases. Ongoing studies examine the atomic structure of the catalysts, optimizing active sites and clarifying the mechanisms behind their biocatalytic actions.

INDEX

SECTION 1 NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY PROFILE

SECTION 2 NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), founded in 1991, is a public research university in Singapore. With over 7,000 employees and 33,000 students, it generates approximately $1.5 billion in annual revenue. Renowned for engineering, business, and sciences, NTU ranks among Asias top universities, excelling in AI, robotics, and sustainability research. Its campus hosts the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine and cutting-edge facilities like the Smart Campus initiative. In 2024, NTU expanded its green energy research, focusing on hydrogen technologies. Competing with the National University of Singapore, NTUs mission is to drive innovation and global impact through interdisciplinary education and transformative research, fostering industry partnerships and societal contributions.

value.Title: Insight Global Invests In Renewable Energy Through New Solar Partnership

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-F7J3-RWVY-M2G5-00000-00Insight Global Invests In Renewable Energy Through New Solar Partnership2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisInsight Global Invests In Renewable Energy Through New Solar Partnership

The company's first direct clean energy initiative marks its continued commitment to sustainability and community impact.
RICHMOND, Va., May 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Insight Global, an international talent and consulting company, announced today it has made investments in renewable energy with the installation of a rooftop solar energy system in Richmond, Virginia. The project has been operational since early spring of this year and is Insight Global's first direct clean energy initiative in the company's overall climate strategy.


Continue Reading
The installation includes a 112.89-kilowatt solar array made up of 210 panels, expected to generate approximately 156,647 kilowatt-hours of renewable electricity annually. The energy will be used to help power Insight Global's day-to-day operations at the Monument Consulting office, an Insight Global subsidiary located on Summit Avenue.
"We're making this investment because it's the right move for our business and the right move for the future," said Jenny Sabo, Senior Vice President of Communications, Benefits, & ESG. "Our partnership with Cherry Street Energy allows us to step into renewable energy in a way that's both operationally smart and financially sound."
Insight Global operates primarily in leased office spaces, which traditionally can limit control over building infrastructure. Through a strong partnership with the building's owner, who is also the founder of Monument Consulting, the company was able to move forward with this meaningful investment.
"As IG increased our attention on sustainability and solutions, I realized I had a unique opportunity to support the company through my ownership of Monument Consulting's office building and the ability to evaluate solar energy generation from our rooftop," said Matt Aprahamian, Founder of Monument Consulting. "We are implementing a solution that will have a significant impact on our organization and our communities."
This solar energy system was financed and installed in partnership with Atlanta-based power provider Cherry Street Energy, who entered into a 20-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with Insight Global.
"We welcome Insight Global into our distributed power network," said Michael Chanin, founder and CEO of Cherry Street Energy. "Whether it's schools, manufacturers, the World's Busiest Airport, or one of the largest talent and consulting companies in the U.S., our platform helps customers control energy market variability with reliable, affordable electricity."
As Insight Global continues to advance its own sustainability initiatives, the company also partners with customers to deliver talent and consulting solutions that support their environmental goals. Examples of this consulting work include:
Partnering with large, multi-site retailers to reduce waste and environmental impact tied to operational disruptions while protecting business continuity and controlling costs. Rethinking packaging for consumer technology companies by building dedicated packaging design engineering teams that deliver a premium customer experience while advancing sustainability objectives. Supporting a major telecom company's Regulated Waste Management (RWM) program to strengthen environmental compliance and reduce operational risk across installation yards and field operations. These efforts have successfully diverted thousands of regulated materials from improper disposal, helping keep harmful waste out of landfills and local ecosystems.About Insight Global:
Insight Global is an international talent and consulting company that delivers business outcomes in an ever-changing world. We obsess over solving problems and building solutions that move our customers further, faster.
With access to top talent in more than 50 countries, our tech-enabled recruiters can build teams quickly. Our technical experts across Cloud, AI, Data, Enterprise Operations, and Applied Engineering deliver solutions tailored to each customer's needs. As those needs evolve, so do we.
As we evolve, though, we stay true to our purpose: to develop our people personally, professionally, and financially so they can be the light to the world around them. It shows up in everything we do, from investing in our people to delivering results for our customers to making a meaningful impact in our communities.
SOURCE Insight Global

value.Title: Balcony solar bill dies in Illinois after union voices opposition

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-XTM3-RRVT-34P0-00000-00Balcony solar bill dies in Illinois after union voices opposition2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisBalcony solar bill dies in Illinois after union voices opposition

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Kari Lydersen

May 8th, 2026 ( Canary Media - Delivered by Newstex )

Lawmakers backing an Illinois bill that would clear the way for balcony solar have ended their push to pass the measure this year. With three weeks still to go in the legislative session, they hit a stalemate as opponents, including the state's powerful electrical workers union, raised concerns about safety.

Advocates and lawmakers who had championed the bill were unhappy with an amendment filed on April 24 that would have prohibited the small plug-in solar panels until national standards are updated to include them.

Supporters say that other states have passed balcony solar laws without that provision. They contend that plugging in a small solar panel is no more dangerous than plugging in a washing machine or hair dryer.

'The whole point of the bill is to make these things safe,' said Kady McFadden, an Illinois lobbyist who focuses on clean energy and was leading legislative strategy for Senate Bill 3104. 'It's finding the right pieces to make sure consumers are safe, and also balancing that with being able to deploy these things.'

Given the amendment and concerns from stakeholders including the union, the lawmakers sponsoring the bill decided against further action, according to McFadden.

Environmental and clean energy groups have worked closely with lawmakers in Illinois and around the country to promote plug-in solar as a way to help people lower their energy bills and access small-scale renewable energy technology. Utah passed the country's first balcony solar bill in 2025, and Maine and Colorado followed suit this spring. More than half the states nationwide are considering balcony solar bills, and legislators have sent them to governors' desks in Maryland and Virginia.

The Illinois bill specified that interconnection agreements from utilities would not be necessary for plug-in solar arrays of up to 1,200 watts, and that landlords and homeowners' associations could not enact barriers to arrays of 391 watts or smaller. Balcony solar systems worldwide typically range from 200 watts to 1,600 watts.

An amendment filed in February would have prohibited any plug-in solar systems greater than 391 watts until the National Electrical Code, a widely used set of safety standards, is updated to include these arrays - a move that is not scheduled until late 2028. The April amendment would have barred any plug-in solar at all until those standards are amended.

'The wait until that code update could be years in the future,' said Kavi Chintam, Illinois campaign manager for the advocacy group Vote Solar. Chintam noted that the bill would have required plug-in solar arrays to be certified by UL Solutions (formerly Underwriters Laboratories), which sets safety standards for appliances and other household goods, or an equivalent organization.

In the state Senate, 139 residents or stakeholder groups filed ' witness slips' in support of the balcony solar bill, while three filed in opposition, including the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers state conference. The representative who filed the IBEW's slip did not respond to requests for comment for this story, nor did two IBEW local chapters.

'We were disappointed to see the opposition' from electricians, said McFadden, noting that a balcony solar bill recently unanimously passed the state Senate in New York, 'which also is a labor-heavy state.'

Bill supporters believed they wouldn't be able to reach consensus on a measure that would allow Illinoisans to quickly adopt balcony solar, so they decided to wait for a future legislative session, McFadden said.

IBEW representatives also raised concerns about the safety of plug-in solar in Oregon, where a bill this spring failed to pass before the session ended.

McFadden said that during the legislative debate, the union representatives didn't bring up fears about how the arrays might impact workers' jobs, but proponents think IBEW may be concerned that plug-in solar does not require an electrician for installation. Chintam said advocates hope to reassure union members that balcony solar would not cut into demand for rooftop solar, which creates jobs for installers and electricians.

'This is a completely different market than what rooftop solar does,' Chintam said. 'It would be small potatoes in terms of the kilowatts that rooftop solar brings. Obviously, we care about the workforce part of solar, but this is a totally different category meant to be purely for appliance-level savings.'

McFadden noted that in the past, it's taken more than one legislative session to enact clean energy bills, most recently the 2025 Clean and Reliable Grid Act as well as sweeping bills that passed in 2021 and 2016.

'Illinois is unique - we like to do things our own way in the legislative process,' said McFadden. 'It takes more than a year to pass a bill.'

urn:contentItem:6JK1-STW3-RSH2-M2P0-00000-00Top Workplaces 2026: The best large companies to work for in Colorado2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisBUSINESSTop Workplaces 2026: The best large companies to work for in ColoradoSpecial to The Denver Post

Editor's note: This information was taken from the Energage database. The businesses provide the data, and it may have changed since it was first entered into the database.

No. 1

Luminate Bank

Years named: 1

Founded: 2021

Headquarters: Minneapolis/St.Paul, Minnesota

Employees: 754, with 56 in Colorado

Facts: Luminate Bank provides nationwide banking, specialized business services, and comprehensive mortgage lending. Formerly known as Equity Bank, it focuses on digital-forward banking, offering high-yield savings, CDs, and tailored commercial/title industry services.

No. 2

Elevations Credit Union

Years named: 4

Founded: 1952

Headquarters: Boulder

Employees: 544

Locations: 19

Facts: Elevations is an award-winning Colorado financial institution with over $3 billion in assets and over 170,000 members.

No. 3

LONG Building Technologies

Years named: 12

Founded: 1965

Headquarters: Littleton

Employees: 626, with 338 in Colorado

Locations: 5 in Colorado

Facts: LONG Building utilizes building technology services to automate, enhance efficiency, make facilities greener, and ensure safety for commercial, institutional, and industrial facilities of all types and sizes.

No. 4

Simpson Housing

Years named: 3, 2 consecutive

Founded: 1948

Headquarters: Denver

Employees: 629, with 133 in Colorado

Facts: Simpson Housing is an experienced, fully integrated real estate firm that provides services in commercial and multifamily property management, development, and construction.

No. 5

Enverus

Years named: 2

Founded: 1999

Headquarters: Austin, Texas

Employees: 1,596, with 103 in Colorado

Facts: Enverus is a SaaS company that uses generative AI to deliver analytics and insights, providing benchmark cost and revenue data from over 95% of U.S. energy producers. It enhances production, distribution, and renewable energy development, serving over 6,000 customers in 50 countries.

No. 6

ENSCO

Years named: 3, 2 consecutive

Founded: 1969

Headquarters: Vienna, Virginia

Employees: 761, with 155 in Colorado

Locations: 2 in Colorado

Facts: ENSCO, Inc. is a technology company with $180 million in revenue. It provides engineering and technology solutions in aerospace, national security, and surface transportation, with a focus on safety and reliability.

No. 7

Solari Crisis & Human Services

Years named: 1

Founded: 2007

Headquarters: Tempe, Arizona

Employees: 796, with 153 in Colorado

Facts: Solari Crisis & Human Services operates 24/7 crisis hotlines and the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, and offers behavioral health services. Known formerly as Crisis Response Network, it specializes in rapid crisis call response, mobile crisis team dispatch, and 911 diversion services in Arizona, Oklahoma, and Colorado.

No. 8

Pinnacol Assurance

Years named: 10

Founded: 1915

Headquarters: Denver

Employees: 633

Facts: Pinnacol Assurance provides workers' compensation protection to more than 50,000 Colorado employers and their employees.

No. 9

Stanley Consultants

Years named: 2, 1 consecutive

Founded: 1913

Headquarters: Muscatine, Iowa

Employees: 900, with 152 in Colorado

Facts: Stanley Consultants is a global, employee-owned firm providing engineering, environmental, and construction services

No. 10

Edward Jones

Years named: 14

Founded: 1922

Headquarters: St. Louis, Mo.

Employees: 55,000, with 959 in Colorado

Locations: 365 in Colorado

Facts: Edward Jones has nearly 20,000 financial advisors who work directly with 9 million clients and $2.2 trillion in client assets.

No. 11

Catholic Charities of Denver

Years named: 2

Founded: 1927

Headquarters: Denver

Employees: 550

Facts: Catholic Charities, Archdiocese of Denver, provides services to 50,000 people through 30 programs in northern Colorado. It focuses on defending human dignity and caring for children, families, and those in need.

No. 12

Breckenridge Grand Vacations

Years named: 12, 7 consecutive

Founded: 1985

Headquarters: Breckenridge

Employees: 700

Locations: 7 in Colorado

Facts: Breckenridge Grand Vacations is a family-owned company that builds, sells, and manages fractional resorts in Breckenridge.

No. 13

Flexential

Years named: 5, 1 consecutive

Founded: 1999

Headquarters: Centennial

Employees: 930

Locations: 5 in Colorado

Facts: Flexential is a leading provider of hybrid IT infrastructure services, offering colocation, cloud, connectivity, and managed solutions across a nationwide data center footprint.

No. 14

Thompson Thrift

Years named: 3

Founded: 1986

Headquarters: Indianapolis

Employees: 675, with 71 in Colorado

Facts: Thompson Thrift is a full-service real estate company that develops multifamily, mixed-use, retail, industrial, and commercial projects across the nation.

No. 15

AllHealth Network

Years named: 8

Founded: 1955

Headquarters: Englewood

Employees: 900, with 650 in Colorado

Locations: 14 in Colorado

Facts: AllHealth Network provides behavioral health care to more than 20,000 children, adults, older adults, families, and couples. Its services include counseling, psychiatry, crisis services, substance abuse treatment, an adult acute treatment unit, services offered to the criminal justice system, vocational and social rehabilitation, school-based services, and an on-site pharmacy.

No. 16

Ping Identity Corp

Years named: 10, 1 consecutive

Founded: 2002

Headquarters: Englewood

Employees: 1,975, with 339 in Colorado

Facts: Ping Identity is an enterprise-grade identity and access management (IAM) software company that provides secure, intelligent authentication for employees, partners, and customers.

No. 17

Coalfire

Years named: 7, 1 consecutive

Founded: 2001

Headquarters: Westminster

Employees: 1,000, with 100 in Colorado

Facts: Coalfire is a premier cybersecurity advisory and assessment firm established in 2001, specializing in cloud security, risk management, and compliance for technology, healthcare, and financial industries.

No. 18

Waterton

Years named: 1

Founded: 1995

Headquarters: Chicago

Employees: 930, with 53 in Colorado

Facts: Waterton is a real estate investment and property management company with a focus on U.S. multifamily and hospitality properties.

No. 19

Colorado State University Global

Years named: 1

Founded: 2007

Headquarters: Denver

Employees: 715

Facts: CSU Global is a public, not-for-profit, 100% online state university. It is a legally independent, third campus of the Colorado State University System, which also includes CSU in Fort Collins and CSU Pueblo

No. 20

Loenbro

Years named: 13, 1 consecutive

Founded: 2000

Headquarters: Westminster

Employees: 4,000, with 927 in Colorado

Facts: Loenbro is an industrial construction and specialized services provider offering comprehensive solutions, including industrial construction, electrical, mechanical, fabrication, and inspection services for industries such as energy, mining, and manufacturing.

No. 21

Vectra Bank Colorado

Years named: 5

Founded: 1998

Headquarters: Denver

Employees: 530

Locations: 35

Facts: Vectra serves Colorado's small and middle-market businesses and their owners, managers, and employees through full-service branches.

No. 22

GOAL Academy High School

Years named: 4

Founded: 2009

Headquarters: Pueblo

Employees: 680

Facts: GOAL High School delivers innovative blended online learning experiences and provides cutting-edge technology to more than 6,000 students across 40 locations statewide.

No. 23

Constant Contact

Years named: 1

Founded: 1995

Headquarters: Waltham, Massachusetts

Employees: 990, with 143 in Colorado

Facts: Constant Contact is a comprehensive digital marketing platform designed primarily for small businesses and nonprofits to create, send, and track email marketing campaigns.

No. 24

Brilliant Earth

Years named: 1

Founded: 2005

Headquarters: San Francisco

Employees: 725, with 61 in Colorado

Facts: Brilliant Earth is a publicly traded jewelry company that specializes in ethically sourced, high-quality engagement rings, wedding bands, and fine jewelry.

Top Workplaces 2026.

value.Title: May 08, 2026: - CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. STATIC WORDS 'CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. AND PEERS: CURATED NEWS ROUNDUP'

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ3-1DW3-RRP3-B34X-00000-00May 08, 2026: - CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. STATIC WORDS 'CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. AND PEERS: CURATED NEWS ROUNDUP'2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 08, 2026: - CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. STATIC WORDS 'CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. AND PEERS: CURATED NEWS ROUNDUP'

Consigli Construction Co. is currently managing the Williams College Museum of Art project, marking the museum's first purpose-built facility on campus. The project, which began on February 25, 2026, aims to enhance student access to art collections. Abigail Berkebile, a figure at Consigli Construction Co., noted the complexity and unique design of the project, stating that the thrill of witnessing the museum staff's enthusiasm as the building progresses is particularly rewarding. She emphasized the importance of awareness regarding diverse opportunities available for women in the construction industry, noting that project teams rely on expertise in accounting, IT, legal matters, marketing, procurement, quality control, and design. To maintain focus, Berkebile highlighted her routine of organizing her calendar and setting aside time to connect with her team daily to ensure alignment on project goals.

In related sector developments, Suffolk Construction is progressing on the Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project. On May 5, 2026, the Virginia Department of Transportation will reopen the right lane on U.S. Route 58 westbound near downtown Suffolk, allowing three lanes of traffic to flow freely. The lane closure coincided with Suffolk Construction's work to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access a regional landfill without crossing westbound lanes. The speed limit through the construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, with cameras monitoring compliance, and some temporary overnight closures may still occur.

Suffolk Construction is also involved in a significant renewable energy initiative, as Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement with Egg Power on May 1, 2026. The agreement will allow the telecommunications company to source approximately 5 percent of its total energy demands from Egg's 70-megawatt solar farm located in Suffolk, which is anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at Virgin Media O2, stated, "This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2's journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We're committed to growing and operating our business in a way that's good for people and the planet."

Turner Construction Company reached a key milestone on April 28, 2026, by celebrating the topping out of the 450,000-square-foot, 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland. The project logged over 80,000 craft hours with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company, remarked, "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame. It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner's commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."

On the same day, April 28, 2026, Turner Construction commenced vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18, with an expected opening in the first quarter of 2028. David Kaminski, Vice President and Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, "This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania." The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact.

Gilbane Development also announced a strategic leadership update on April 28, 2026, appointing Andreas Vlahakis as the new head of capital markets in its New York City office. Vlahakis, who previously served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, is tasked with refining the firm's funding strategy, leading transaction structuring, and developing relationships with funding partners. He will report directly to James Patchett, president and CEO, focusing on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

Additionally, Gilbane is moving forward with a public-private partnership at Western Kentucky University to design and develop a new residence hall. The partnership received formal approval from the university's Board of Regents on December 11, 2025, and subsequent approval from the Kentucky Legislature on April 6, 2026. Jakob Barker, a university student government representative, confirmed the authorization, stating, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." This development addresses housing needs by replacing older facilities and reflects ongoing efforts to enhance campus resources.

value.Title: Dominion announces plans for new 3-gigawatt gas plant with electricity demand projected to surge

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-X763-SBV3-919M-00000-00Dominion announces plans for new 3-gigawatt gas plant with electricity demand projected to surge2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:49ZLexisNexisNEWSDominion announces plans for new 3-gigawatt gas plant with electricity demand projected to surge

Dominion Energy is aiming to expand their portfolio of energy sources in Virginia with a massive 3,000 megawatt (3 gigawatts) combined cycle natural gas plant in Cumberland County, west of Richmond. The utility is now preparing to seek state and local regulators' approval of the plant, slated to be operational in less than 10 years.

The plant will produce base load energy around the clock and will be able to provide power for up to 750,000 homes. If approved, it will be, by far, the largest natural gas facility in Dominion's .

The proposed facility will outsize the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, now in its and slated to produce 2,600 MW of power when complete. The company said the new project is necessary to meet soaring energy needs statewide.

"PJM (the regional electric grid operator) is forecasting that our demand will grow on average by about 5% annually over the next 20 years and more than doubling by 2045," Jeremy Slayton, a representative for Dominion, said Thursday. "To meet this unprecedented demand growth reliably and affordably, we are going to need to generate twice as much energy to keep up with that demand into the future."

The utility is just beginning the application process for the plant, planned to be located on 900 acres on Duncan Store Road in the northern region of Cumberland, bordering the James River.

Before the plans can move forward, the project will have to receive approval from the county through a conditional use permit, get the green light from the State Corporation Commission and secure a Department of Environmental Quality air permit.

This process is expected to take about two years with construction anticipated to start in 2029. It will afford several opportunities for the public to voice their opinions on the plant, as energy costs and reliability remain a top concern for residents.

Dominion's recent projections show that in order to meet the growing energy demands in the region, the company will have to build an estimated six natural gas plants, on top of other renewable energy sources, over the next 20 years. These estimates have met with significant public criticism and concerns about environmental impacts.

In Dominion's , where the company must explain to the SCC their long-range energy production roadmap, the utility estimated that solar will be its largest growth sector, representing 53% of new energy sources. Natural gas is the second largest with 25%, wind will comprise 10%, battery storage will be 6% and small nuclear reactors will be 6%.

Last month, the SCC for five solar and battery storage projects and 10 power purchase agreements totaling up to a gigawatt of power.

"What matters most to our customers is that when they go home and they flip that light switch, the power comes on," Slayton said. "We are pursuing projects like the Cumberland Energy Center to ensure that we are providing a reliable and affordable grid and affordable and reliable energy for our customers."

The full cost of the new plant is yet to be determined, but Dominion said it will be a "multi-billion dollar project." Slayton confirmed that it will cost more than the (CERC), a 944 MW gas peaker plant that is priced at $1.47 billion.

The CERC project faced years of due to concerns over health impacts from the particulates that would be released into the air from the plant, as well as a push for more renewable energy sources to be utilized over fossil fuels.

Compared to other natural gas plants such as their facilities in Greenville and Brunswick, Dominion said, the technology underpinning the new plant has advanced.

The Cumberland plant would include two sets of two gas turbines paired with a steam turbine that captures the residual energy from burning the gas, which will also be used for power generation.

The turbines will be hydrogen-capable, should that be a useful future energy source. A hydrogen recycling plant is operational in Petersburg and the state is working to build out the industry.

"Because the life of these assets is over 30 years, we want the units to be as capable to be able to accept alternate fuels when and if they come available," said Brandon Martin, Dominion's director of generation development. "In the event that the future says hydrogen is now economic or viable for our plants, we'd like them to be able to accept hydrogen."

Dominion estimates the project will create 450 local construction and operation jobs and will generate about $500 million in local tax revenue over the life of the project.

If permits are approved, the company anticipates the plant will come online in 2033 or 2034.

Dominion is also beholden to the Virginia Clean Economy Act that mandates the retirement of carbon emission energy production by 2045, unless the SCC approves a plant to remain in service to ensure grid reliability.

"I think as the SCC signaled with the approval of CERC late last year, there is a reliability need in the future," Slayton said.

The utility mailed letters to residents who live around the proposed site for the project with details about the plant. There will be two informational meetings in the county with Dominion representatives later this month and in June.

Once the conditional use permit for the project is submitted to the county board of supervisors, it will later be scheduled for public hearings and a vote.

value.Title: MARS, INCORPORATED - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-JGT3-S2F2-P4B5-00000-00MARS, INCORPORATED - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisSTOCKMARS, INCORPORATED - PEER SECTOR NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: MARS, INCORPORATED

PermID5040171391Websitehttp://www.mars.comIndustryPackaged Foods & Confectionery

ACTIVITIES:

Mars, Incorporated, founded in 1911, is a family-owned global confectionery and pet care company headquartered in McLean, Virginia. Employing over 140,000 people across 80 countries, it generates over $45 billion annually. Known for brands like M&Ms, Snickers, and Pedigree, Mars operates in confectionery, pet care, and food. In 2024, it acquired Kellanova for $35.9 billion, expanding its snack portfolio. Its culture, guided by Five Principles (Quality, Responsibility, Mutuality, Efficiency, Freedom), emphasizes sustainability and employee well-being. Competing with Nestl, Mars stands out for its private ownership and sustainability goals, targeting net-zero emissions by 2050. Recent initiatives include investments in plant-based foods and digital supply chain solutions.

+ MARS, INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ MARS SECURES MAJOR CLEAN ENERGY PACT IN LITHUANIA

On April 28, 2026, Mars, Incorporated announced a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for the majority output from the Skuodas Wind Farm in Lithuania. The deal is expected to generate about 490 GWh of renewable electricity annually and reduce carbon emissions by approximately 120,000 tons per year. The Skuodas Wind Farm has an installed capacity of 158.4 MW and will supply renewable energy to Mars' consumption and value chain, supporting the Renewables Acceleration Program and the company's net-zero ambitions.Kevin Rabinovitch, Global VP of Sustainability at Mars, called the arrangement a milestone for real-world climate action, noting that "this agreement with European Energy helps bring new wind power online in Lithuania and strengthens our ability to extend credible renewable electricity across our value chain." The project is scheduled to go live in 2028 and will reinforce Mars' Lithuania manufacturing facility with a reliable source of renewable electricity, while also expanding domestic wind capacity in Europe through the corporate PPA.

+ MARS INC BROADENS RENEWABLE FOOTPRINT WITH LITHUANIAN WIND PPA

Mars Inc has signed a wind power purchase agreement to power its Lithuanian pet food factory, signaling a firm step toward sustainable operations. The PPA was finalized on 28th April 2026, as Mars moves to shift its Lithuanian operations to greener energy and aligns with its plan to reach net zero greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2050.By tapping wind energy, Mars aims to reduce emissions and set an example for the pet food sector. Lithuania has been expanding renewable capacity, reaching about 6 GW of combined wind and solar generation, and the wind PPA comes as European firms boost renewable investments.The Lithuanian plant is a key link in Mars European supply chain, producing popular pet nutrition brands. The deal is expected to improve ecological efficiency and could attract investors who value sustainability, while signaling Mars leadership in responsible manufacturing.

+ MARS LAUNCHES PROTECT THE PEANUT CAMPAIGN TO SAFEGUARD M&M'S PEANUT QUALITY

Mars Inc. has rolled out a global Protect the Peanut campaign for M&M's across social channels, kicking off on 7th April 2026, and featuring a short documentary by BBDO Chicago that addresses consumer concerns about Peanut M&M's and points to peanut quality rather than candy flaws. Peanuts are notoriously vulnerable to disease and weather, with roughly 30% of crops failing before harvest. For M&M's, only about 1 in 100 peanuts meets their exacting quality standard. Mars says the effort blends securing the crop's future with launching a hardier peanut variety, backed by roughly $10 million invested over a decade in research with scientists from the University of Georgia. The initiative also includes additional funding—about $12 million in premiums—to help farmers transition to the new peanut type. Early indicators suggest Peanut M&M's complaints have fallen by around 90%. As BBDO’s Josh Gross put it, "Sometimes the most powerful ideas start with someone noticing something small and then starting to ask the right questions," underscoring the campaign's aim to address core issues and protect a beloved product.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) LAUNCHES SNACK NA SWAK INITIATIVE TO PROMOTE MINDFUL FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondelez International Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) launched its Snack na Swak initiative on Apr. 27, 2026, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos by encouraging mindful choices within a recommended 250-calorie range advised by local health authorities. The campaign treats snacking as a cultural touchpoint, from quick merienda between meetings to shared family moments, and promotes mindful snacking as an opportunity to balance portions, nutrition, and the overall experience. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, called for clearer labeling and more portion-controlled options to aid healthier decisions. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VICE PRESIDENT – MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing, with Viswanathan having spent nearly eight years at Mondelez and most recently serving as Senior Director of Marketing. In his new role, he will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with India and Global Technology Leadership. Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008, later serving as VP Marketing – Chocolates at Cadbury Kraft India from 2011 to 2013. He joined Mondelez International in 2014, relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, and returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020. The promotion comes as Mondelez International focuses on strengthening its market position in India amid a digital-forward consumer landscape and ongoing emphasis on innovative marketing strategies

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) GETS UPGRADED BY BOFA ON POSITIVE MANAGEMENT TALKS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) attracted investor attention after Bank of America raised its price target on the snack maker to $65 from $62 and kept a Buy rating, citing positive management updates from meetings in March. On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its constructive stance and flagged ongoing discussions with European chocolate retailers as a potential growth avenueUBS trimmed its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 and maintained a Neutral rating, with the bank expecting first-quarter results across consumer staples to show some improvement while warning that inflation could weigh on earnings in the second half of 2026Mondelez, which produces chocolate, biscuits and baked snacks, also has exposure to gum, candy, cheese, grocery items and powdered beverages. The stock is among 13 NASDAQ names with the highest dividends, adding a yield-oriented angle for investors evaluating the name

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER PLC (LSE:ULVR) WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) is weighing a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to sharpen growth in its beauty, personal care and wellbeing brands. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions said the group is engaging advisers to assess options, including a potential divestment of the food business, which includes Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes. Deliberations are in early stages, with scenarios ranging from a full spin-off to retaining flagship brands while divesting others; a formal decision is not expected before 2027. Industry insiders peg a deal value in the tens of billions of dollars if pursued, though no path has been finalized. Unilever's food arm includes Colman's condiments and Marmite, and the stock has traded about 0.7% lower, placing the company's market capitalization around £107 billion ($142 billion). Under CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is refocusing on its beauty and wellbeing brands, having spun off the ice cream division last year while retaining a minority stake to be sold gradually. Analysts such as Warren Ackerman at Barclays note the direction toward beauty is clear, but timing for a food separation may be sensitive given market conditions. The group has previously divested the global spreads division and snack brands, leaving roughly €1 billion to €1.5 billion of local food brands to exit

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) AI-DRIVEN INNOVATION ACCELERATES BEAUTY AND WELLBEING DEVELOPMENT

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) On 28th April 2026, the company disclosed that it is leveraging artificial intelligence to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling products to be created in days rather than months. Its €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is deploying AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, drawing from more than 1,000 external data sources each month to guide product development. Unique AI insights tools synthesize findings with the company's extensive R&D databases, streamlining the formulation process and reducing development cycles from five or six rounds to one or two. The time to move from concept to market has shrunk from months to days, and the claims generation process is 75% quicker, boosting overall efficiency.Beyond faster formulation, the company taps its R&D datasets to explore biological data and run complex research at unprecedented speeds. The introduction of virtual cohorts enables scientists to simulate demographic responses to new products, cutting time and costs for early-stage testing without forgoing real-world trials. AI-driven innovations have already led to launches such as the Hydra Miracle range, which uses a groundbreaking ingredient to enhance skin hydration. The Damage Therapy range is delivering double-digit growth, underscoring how AI supports both efficiency and scientific discovery. As the Chief R&D Officer notes, structured data alongside human creativity is redefining the possibilities in research and development.

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) RECEIVES MCCORMICK OFFER VALUED AT €29 BILLION

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) receives an offer from McCormick & Co. for its food division, valued at about €29 billion ($33 billion). The parties disclosed the approach on Apr. 26, 2026, with Unilever confirming discussions are underway and that terms remain undisclosed. The talks signal the potential reshaping of both groups, though no binding agreement has been reached

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS, INC. (NYSE:GIS) EXPANDS OMNICHANNEL PROGRAM TO STRENGTHEN GROWTH TRAJECTORY

General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) is positioning omnichannel execution as a central pillar of its Remarkable Experience framework, underscoring its role in driving more consistent consumer engagement across digital and physical retail environments. Apr. 14, 2026 marked a demonstration that its focus on online and offline integration is delivering tangible results.A primary driver has been General Mills' ability to leverage its portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to achieve differential retail execution. The company has ramped up investments in retail media to capture consumers who prioritize convenience alongside value. This targeted approach to winning online has yielded a tripling of its e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners. The omnichannel momentum is corroborated by seven of the top ten North America retail categories showing rising household penetration through the fiscal third quarter.

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.

+ GENERAL MILLS REFRESHES CEREAL LINE TO ALIGN WITH CONSUMER TRENDS

General Mills on 7th April 2026 rolled out two cereal innovations, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney's Moana, part of a broader cereal refresh that aims to remove artificial colors and broaden flavor options through brand collaborations.General Mills traded at 37.67 dollars, about 7.9 percent below the consensus target of 40.89, with recent results showing weakness in the stock, including a 14.9 percent drop over the last 30 days and a 51.2 percent decline over three years. The cereal launches are described as part of a longer term strategy to rebuild brand relevance and consumer engagement, even as earnings are forecast to fall about 6.3 percent annually. The company also confronts valuation questions, as shares sit well below estimated fair value with a price-to-earnings ratio near 9.1 and debt levels that raise questions about cash-flow sufficiency.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HERSHEY COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) SETS SOURCE. MAKE. DELIGHT. SUSTAINABILITY FRAMEWORK

On 27th April 2026, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) Sets its new Source. Make. Delight. framework to embed sustainability into its core operations rather than treating it as an ancillary task, a response to climate change, supply chain pressures, and evolving consumer expectationsWhitney Mayer, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) Head of Global Sustainability, says the previous goals often targeted 2040 or 2050 and lacked the operational focus for immediate impact. The Source. Make. Delight. framework centers on three pillars: source strengthens resilient supply chains and cocoa sourcing for farmers; make pursues operational efficiency, waste reduction, and energy improvements; delight connects sustainability with consumer experience through transparent storytelling and data-driven decision-making to monitor progressBy aligning sustainability initiatives with business performance, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) aims to build a more resilient and efficient operation

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) EXPANDS BREAKFAST PORTFOLIO WITH CRUNCHY COOKIE CHOCOLATE SPREAD THROUGH HERSHEY INDIA

The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) through its subsidiary Hershey India Pvt Ltd. launched its Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread as part of its strategy to expand breakfast offerings in India, on 24th April 2026. The new Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread blends the rich flavor of creamy chocolate with the texture of crunchy cookies, targeting Millennials and Gen Z consumers who seek innovative and sensory experiences during breakfast. Hershey India's marketing campaign features the hashtag #TwoGoodTogether to highlight the product's appeal through relatable storytelling.Hershey India positions itself to promote togetherness, aligning with Indian cultural values that emphasize family and shared meals. By expanding its portfolio to include the Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread, Hershey India aims to enhance its existing offerings, which include variants of its popular chocolate spreads. Ankit Desai, Marketing Director at Hershey India, stated, "As a leading premium chocolate brand, we are committed to meeting the evolving needs of our consumers with innovative products. The launch of the \"Crunchy Cookie\" chocolate spread reflects our focus on delivering unique and enjoyable breakfast options for our customers."

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) LAUNCHES CONNECTED WORKER PROGRAM TO MODERNIZE MANUFACTURING

The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) launched its Connected Worker program on the 20th of April 2026, as part of its digital transformation in manufacturing, to empower factory teams with mobile tools for real-time insights, accelerated training, and improved communications across the production lineThe initiative replaces traditional paper checklists and manuals with digital tools. Operators access production data, training resources, and colleagues via mobile devices, ensuring knowledge is shared across shifts and plants. Generative AI helps create instructional content, and QR codes on equipment can surface relevant training materials instantlyMaintenance teams and supervisors gain clearer visibility into maintenance requests and performance metrics, enabling faster prioritization and more coaching time for leaders. Hershey aims to roll out the Connected Worker program to all manufacturing facilities, including salty snacks and confection factories, within 18 months

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ J.M. SMUCKER [3 News That Matters]

+ THE J.M. SMUCKER COMPANY (NYSE:SJM) COFFEE STRENGTH OUTPACES SNACK WEAKNESS IN INVESTMENT UPDATE

The J.M. Smucker Company (NYSE:SJM) faces a split trajectory as strong coffee results contrast with weakness in Sweet Baked Snacks, shaping a nuanced investment narrative. Wells Fargo analyst Chris Carey reaffirmed an Overweight rating while trimming sector estimates on inflation driven cost pressures and a cautious earnings outlook

+ THE J.M. SMUCKER COMPANY (NYSE:SJM) POSTS GROWTH ACROSS PET FOOD AND COFFEE AS CONSUMER TRENDS SHIFT

The J.M. Smucker Company (NYSE:SJM) stock is drawing investor interest as it leverages strength in pet food and coffee amid evolving consumer behavior and grocery dynamics. On 13th April 2026, investors weigh whether Smucker's Folgers, Jif, and Milk-Bone can sustain growth against competition and shifting preferences. The J.M. Smucker Company operates through three segments: U.S. Retail Coffee, U.S. Retail Consumer Foods, and U.S. Retail Pet Foods. The coffee segment remains the largest revenue contributor, while pet food is emerging as a key growth driver. The 2020 acquisition of Ainsworth Pet Nutrition strengthens high-quality pet foods amid rising pet humanization, and the Hostess Brands acquisition broadens the product mix. The company backs its strategy with a robust balance sheet and a history of dividend growth—27 consecutive years of increases. Yet challenges linger in the consumer foods space, notably declining peanut butter consumption amid allergy awareness. Looking ahead, quarterly results from pet foods and snacks will be pivotal to offset potential coffee-volume softness, while ongoing regulatory scrutiny and integration risks remain.

+ J.M. SMUCKER UNVEILS 30-YEAR PACKAGING REFRESH FOR SMUCKER’S FRUIT SPREADS

J.M. Smucker has introduced a packaging refresh for Smucker’s fruit spreads, the first major update in almost thirty years. The new jars began arriving in spring, from 6th April 2026, and they lean on a refreshed ’90s-inspired design with bolder colors, bigger fruit imagery, and a broader use of the gingham motif.The gingham pattern, long a staple on the jar lid, now appears on the front label as well. Each flavor will carry a distinct color to improve on-shelf identification, while the recipe and ingredients remain unchanged. Dayna Lewallen, senior design manager, creative and design at The J.M. Smucker Company, said, "This redesign was about honoring the Smucker’s brand’s most iconic assets and evolving them with modern intention."The refresh aligns with a broader trend of ’90s nostalgia in food and consumer culture. Smucker’s is using the redesign to modernize its shelf presence and help the spreads stand out in a category where visuals matter for trial, while signaling expanded usage occasions such as breakfast, snacking, charcuterie boards, and yogurt bowls.For retailers, the transition will roll out over the coming months with no reformulation or ingredient changes. The visual enhancements are designed to help consumers identify flavors quickly and give Smucker’s a more contemporary presence in the spreads aisle.Headquartered in Orrville, Ohio, J.M. Smucker leads major grocery categories including coffee, peanut butter and fruit spreads, with a portfolio that includes Folgers, Dunkin’, Cafe Bustelo, Jif, Uncrustables, Hostess, Milk-Bone and Meow Mix.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FERRERO INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ TOM BRADY LEADS FERRERO'S $100 MILLION WORLD CUP CAMPAIGN

Tom Brady has secured a partnership with Ferrero North America for the brand's World Cup campaign, announced on 30th March 2026. The collaboration places the seven-time Super Bowl champion at the center of Ferrero's 'Go All In' promotion, which marks the company's largest-ever marketing commitment at $100 million.The campaign targets North American audiences as the FIFA World Cup approaches, with Ten Toes playing a key role in identifying Brady and managing the collaboration, underscoring Ferrero's aim to bridge soccer's global appeal with U.S. culture.A commercial featuring Brady is slated to launch across streaming platforms, including Netflix, Amazon and HBO, as well as on social media channels, highlighting Ferrero's effort to reach domestic audiences.

+ FERRERO ANNOUNCES $1 MILLION GO ALL IN SOCCER PROMOTION WITH TOM BRADY

Ferrero International is launching its Go All In promotion tied to the global soccer tournament, featuring NFL legend Tom Brady. The program runs from Apr. 1, 2026 to Jul. 31, 2026 and offers entrants a grand prize of $1 million along with numerous daily and weekly prizes. Customers can enter by purchasing any two Ferrero products or opt for a free entry, making participation accessible to a wide audience. Brady's involvement adds star power as the campaign aims to energize fans across platforms. A 30-second advertisement featuring Brady will air starting Apr. 1, 2026 on Tubi, Netflix and YouTube, highlighting Ferrero's product lineup and the Go All In theme with the goal of driving engagement around soccer. Ferrero has earmarked about $100 million for the campaign, its largest marketing push to date, spanning traditional advertising, digital activations, influencer partnerships and in-store displays. The company will leverage beloved brands such as Nutella, Ferrero Rocher and Butterfinger to reach consumers in North America, supported by Ferrero North America president and chief business officer Michael Lindsey on turning casual shoppers into fans of the full Ferrero portfolio.

+ BARRY CALLEBAUT AND FERRERO LEAD UK COCOA COALITION TO ACCELERATE SUSTAINABLE SOURCING

Major chocolate manufacturers Barry Callebaut, Ferrero, The Hershey Company, and Tony’s Chocolonely have announced their membership in the UK Cocoa Coalition, a collective effort aimed at enhancing sustainability in cocoa supply chains. The coalition, which includes NGOs and retailers, is calling for the swift implementation of the UK Forest Risk Commodities Regulation to curb deforestation linked to cocoa sourcing.The formation comes amid concerns over environmental degradation and human rights violations within the cocoa industry. In a statement dated 26th March 2026, Ferrero UK’s Richard Laming stressed that collaboration is essential, noting that \"one single entity cannot address the challenges the cocoa industry is facing alone.\" The group has aligned with NGOs such as the Rainforest Alliance and the Fairtrade Foundation, and with retailers including Sainsbury’s and Marks & Spencer, signaling a shift toward agreed sustainability baselines rather than competition on ethics.Belinda Borck of Tony’s Chocolonely echoed the call for shared responsibility to address illegal deforestation and improve farmers' livelihoods. The coalition aims to drive policy that can enable systemic change, building a more resilient cocoa industry for farmers and the environment. The announcement marks a notable moment for the sector, as the UK remains a major market for chocolate confectionery.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PEPSICO [3 News That Matters]

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) PARTNERS WITH FERTIBERIA TO DECARBONISE EUROPEAN POTATO AND CORN FARMING

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) Partners With Fertiberia To Decarbonise European Potato And Corn Farming Across Approximately 400,000 Acres. PepsiCo and Fertiberia announced on 5th May 2026 a collaboration to deploy regenerative farming techniques and advanced fertilizers aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions while preserving or boosting crop yields across Europe.The initiative focuses on decarbonisation of the supply chain and aims to improve PepsiCo's ESG profile, with potential benefits for investor perception as MSCI ESG scores evaluate sustainability relative to peers. Improvements in PepsiCo's score could attract environmentally conscious investors. The move follows PepsiCo's prior investments in reducing plastic waste and improving water management, and the effort could strengthen the company's position in sustainable agriculture. Stakeholders will monitor progress for potential signals on environmental impact and the company's financial performance, and the project could set a precedent for future collaborations in sustainable agriculture.

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) POSTS MARGIN BUFFER FROM PRODUCTIVITY PUSH AMID INFLATIONARY PRESSURES

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) is actively pursuing productivity initiatives to shield its performance from the adverse effects of rising inflation. As of 4th May 2026, these efforts have begun to yield positive results, with management outlining a multi-faceted approach to cost efficiencies, supply-chain optimization, and disciplined overhead management.Productivity savings have become a key lever for margin protection. The company reports improvements in supply-chain efficiency, workforce optimization, SKU rationalization, and metrics such as cases processed per hour. Investments in technology, including AI-driven logistics and digital ordering systems, are driving cost reductions and enabling reinvestment in growth areas like marketing, innovation, and value creation.PepsiCo's diversification and global demand underpin resilience, with growth in energy drinks and functional beverages supporting volumes. Still, management says productivity savings won't fully offset inflation; a balanced plan that includes pricing and revenue growth remains necessary. In markets, the stock has declined 6% over the last three months, trading at a forward P/E of 17.87 versus the industry average of 18.89. Zacks Consensus sees earnings rising 5.1% in 2026 and 3.2% in 2027, underscoring cautious optimism as the company executes its productivity agenda

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) SIGNS 10-YEAR VPPA WITH STATKRAFT TO ACCELERATE RENEWABLE ENERGY IN EUROPE

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) Signed a 10-year virtual power purchase agreement with Statkraft to decarbonize its European operations and supply chain. The deal, announced May 4, 2026, will source electricity from a wind project in Spain that is undergoing modernization to boost its output.PepsiCo estimates the clean power will cut about 32,000 metric tons of CO2 emissions annually, according to a release dated Apr. 28. As lead buyer, the company collaborated with Givaudan and Smurfit Westrock to secure favorable terms and access long-term renewable energy opportunities typically reserved for larger buyers. The arrangement is part of PepsiCo's pep+ Renew program, launched with Schneider Electric in 2022 to help supply chain partners transition to renewable electricity.PepsiCo has engaged Schneider Electric SE Advisory Services to structure this VPPA with Statkraft. The deal marks the second pep+ Renew cohort to sign a VPPA and the first European renewable electricity cohort. Archana Jagannathan, PepsiCo's chief sustainability officer for Europe, the Middle East and Africa, emphasized that the agreement expands access to renewable energy and supports the transition to cleaner power. The VPPA aligns with the PepsiCo Positive pep+ program, launched in 2021. In its sustainability strategy reset, PepsiCo moved its net-zero target date from 2040 to 2050, using 2022 as the baseline, after signaling it would likely miss the 2025 targets and setting new packaging sustainability goals and updated emissions reductions for 2030.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
