value.Title: MARS, INCORPORATED - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-TKT3-S1N0-G08P-00000-00MARS, INCORPORATED - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisSTOCKMARS, INCORPORATED - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: MARS, INCORPORATED

PermID5040171391Websitehttp://www.mars.comIndustryPackaged Foods & Confectionery

ACTIVITIES:

Mars, Incorporated, founded in 1911, is a family-owned global confectionery and pet care company headquartered in McLean, Virginia. Employing over 140,000 people across 80 countries, it generates over $45 billion annually. Known for brands like M&Ms, Snickers, and Pedigree, Mars operates in confectionery, pet care, and food. In 2024, it acquired Kellanova for $35.9 billion, expanding its snack portfolio. Its culture, guided by Five Principles (Quality, Responsibility, Mutuality, Efficiency, Freedom), emphasizes sustainability and employee well-being. Competing with Nestl, Mars stands out for its private ownership and sustainability goals, targeting net-zero emissions by 2050. Recent initiatives include investments in plant-based foods and digital supply chain solutions.

+ MARS, INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ MARS SECURES MAJOR CLEAN ENERGY PACT IN LITHUANIA

On April 28, 2026, Mars, Incorporated announced a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for the majority output from the Skuodas Wind Farm in Lithuania. The deal is expected to generate about 490 GWh of renewable electricity annually and reduce carbon emissions by approximately 120,000 tons per year. The Skuodas Wind Farm has an installed capacity of 158.4 MW and will supply renewable energy to Mars' consumption and value chain, supporting the Renewables Acceleration Program and the company's net-zero ambitions.Kevin Rabinovitch, Global VP of Sustainability at Mars, called the arrangement a milestone for real-world climate action, noting that "this agreement with European Energy helps bring new wind power online in Lithuania and strengthens our ability to extend credible renewable electricity across our value chain." The project is scheduled to go live in 2028 and will reinforce Mars' Lithuania manufacturing facility with a reliable source of renewable electricity, while also expanding domestic wind capacity in Europe through the corporate PPA.

+ MARS INC BROADENS RENEWABLE FOOTPRINT WITH LITHUANIAN WIND PPA

Mars Inc has signed a wind power purchase agreement to power its Lithuanian pet food factory, signaling a firm step toward sustainable operations. The PPA was finalized on 28th April 2026, as Mars moves to shift its Lithuanian operations to greener energy and aligns with its plan to reach net zero greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2050.By tapping wind energy, Mars aims to reduce emissions and set an example for the pet food sector. Lithuania has been expanding renewable capacity, reaching about 6 GW of combined wind and solar generation, and the wind PPA comes as European firms boost renewable investments.The Lithuanian plant is a key link in Mars European supply chain, producing popular pet nutrition brands. The deal is expected to improve ecological efficiency and could attract investors who value sustainability, while signaling Mars leadership in responsible manufacturing.

+ MARS LAUNCHES PROTECT THE PEANUT CAMPAIGN TO SAFEGUARD M&M'S PEANUT QUALITY

Mars Inc. has rolled out a global Protect the Peanut campaign for M&M's across social channels, kicking off on 7th April 2026, and featuring a short documentary by BBDO Chicago that addresses consumer concerns about Peanut M&M's and points to peanut quality rather than candy flaws. Peanuts are notoriously vulnerable to disease and weather, with roughly 30% of crops failing before harvest. For M&M's, only about 1 in 100 peanuts meets their exacting quality standard. Mars says the effort blends securing the crop's future with launching a hardier peanut variety, backed by roughly $10 million invested over a decade in research with scientists from the University of Georgia. The initiative also includes additional funding—about $12 million in premiums—to help farmers transition to the new peanut type. Early indicators suggest Peanut M&M's complaints have fallen by around 90%. As BBDO’s Josh Gross put it, "Sometimes the most powerful ideas start with someone noticing something small and then starting to ask the right questions," underscoring the campaign's aim to address core issues and protect a beloved product.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) LAUNCHES SNACK NA SWAK INITIATIVE TO PROMOTE MINDFUL FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondelez International Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) launched its Snack na Swak initiative on Apr. 27, 2026, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos by encouraging mindful choices within a recommended 250-calorie range advised by local health authorities. The campaign treats snacking as a cultural touchpoint, from quick merienda between meetings to shared family moments, and promotes mindful snacking as an opportunity to balance portions, nutrition, and the overall experience. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, called for clearer labeling and more portion-controlled options to aid healthier decisions. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VICE PRESIDENT – MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing, with Viswanathan having spent nearly eight years at Mondelez and most recently serving as Senior Director of Marketing. In his new role, he will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with India and Global Technology Leadership. Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008, later serving as VP Marketing – Chocolates at Cadbury Kraft India from 2011 to 2013. He joined Mondelez International in 2014, relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, and returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020. The promotion comes as Mondelez International focuses on strengthening its market position in India amid a digital-forward consumer landscape and ongoing emphasis on innovative marketing strategies

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) GETS UPGRADED BY BOFA ON POSITIVE MANAGEMENT TALKS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) attracted investor attention after Bank of America raised its price target on the snack maker to $65 from $62 and kept a Buy rating, citing positive management updates from meetings in March. On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its constructive stance and flagged ongoing discussions with European chocolate retailers as a potential growth avenueUBS trimmed its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 and maintained a Neutral rating, with the bank expecting first-quarter results across consumer staples to show some improvement while warning that inflation could weigh on earnings in the second half of 2026Mondelez, which produces chocolate, biscuits and baked snacks, also has exposure to gum, candy, cheese, grocery items and powdered beverages. The stock is among 13 NASDAQ names with the highest dividends, adding a yield-oriented angle for investors evaluating the name

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER PLC (LSE:ULVR) WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) is weighing a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to sharpen growth in its beauty, personal care and wellbeing brands. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions said the group is engaging advisers to assess options, including a potential divestment of the food business, which includes Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes. Deliberations are in early stages, with scenarios ranging from a full spin-off to retaining flagship brands while divesting others; a formal decision is not expected before 2027. Industry insiders peg a deal value in the tens of billions of dollars if pursued, though no path has been finalized. Unilever's food arm includes Colman's condiments and Marmite, and the stock has traded about 0.7% lower, placing the company's market capitalization around £107 billion ($142 billion). Under CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is refocusing on its beauty and wellbeing brands, having spun off the ice cream division last year while retaining a minority stake to be sold gradually. Analysts such as Warren Ackerman at Barclays note the direction toward beauty is clear, but timing for a food separation may be sensitive given market conditions. The group has previously divested the global spreads division and snack brands, leaving roughly €1 billion to €1.5 billion of local food brands to exit

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) AI-DRIVEN INNOVATION ACCELERATES BEAUTY AND WELLBEING DEVELOPMENT

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) On 28th April 2026, the company disclosed that it is leveraging artificial intelligence to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling products to be created in days rather than months. Its €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is deploying AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, drawing from more than 1,000 external data sources each month to guide product development. Unique AI insights tools synthesize findings with the company's extensive R&D databases, streamlining the formulation process and reducing development cycles from five or six rounds to one or two. The time to move from concept to market has shrunk from months to days, and the claims generation process is 75% quicker, boosting overall efficiency.Beyond faster formulation, the company taps its R&D datasets to explore biological data and run complex research at unprecedented speeds. The introduction of virtual cohorts enables scientists to simulate demographic responses to new products, cutting time and costs for early-stage testing without forgoing real-world trials. AI-driven innovations have already led to launches such as the Hydra Miracle range, which uses a groundbreaking ingredient to enhance skin hydration. The Damage Therapy range is delivering double-digit growth, underscoring how AI supports both efficiency and scientific discovery. As the Chief R&D Officer notes, structured data alongside human creativity is redefining the possibilities in research and development.

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) RECEIVES MCCORMICK OFFER VALUED AT €29 BILLION

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) receives an offer from McCormick & Co. for its food division, valued at about €29 billion ($33 billion). The parties disclosed the approach on Apr. 26, 2026, with Unilever confirming discussions are underway and that terms remain undisclosed. The talks signal the potential reshaping of both groups, though no binding agreement has been reached

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS, INC. (NYSE:GIS) EXPANDS OMNICHANNEL PROGRAM TO STRENGTHEN GROWTH TRAJECTORY

General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) is positioning omnichannel execution as a central pillar of its Remarkable Experience framework, underscoring its role in driving more consistent consumer engagement across digital and physical retail environments. Apr. 14, 2026 marked a demonstration that its focus on online and offline integration is delivering tangible results.A primary driver has been General Mills' ability to leverage its portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to achieve differential retail execution. The company has ramped up investments in retail media to capture consumers who prioritize convenience alongside value. This targeted approach to winning online has yielded a tripling of its e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners. The omnichannel momentum is corroborated by seven of the top ten North America retail categories showing rising household penetration through the fiscal third quarter.

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.

+ GENERAL MILLS REFRESHES CEREAL LINE TO ALIGN WITH CONSUMER TRENDS

General Mills on 7th April 2026 rolled out two cereal innovations, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney's Moana, part of a broader cereal refresh that aims to remove artificial colors and broaden flavor options through brand collaborations.General Mills traded at 37.67 dollars, about 7.9 percent below the consensus target of 40.89, with recent results showing weakness in the stock, including a 14.9 percent drop over the last 30 days and a 51.2 percent decline over three years. The cereal launches are described as part of a longer term strategy to rebuild brand relevance and consumer engagement, even as earnings are forecast to fall about 6.3 percent annually. The company also confronts valuation questions, as shares sit well below estimated fair value with a price-to-earnings ratio near 9.1 and debt levels that raise questions about cash-flow sufficiency.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HERSHEY COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) SETS SOURCE. MAKE. DELIGHT. SUSTAINABILITY FRAMEWORK

On 27th April 2026, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) Sets its new Source. Make. Delight. framework to embed sustainability into its core operations rather than treating it as an ancillary task, a response to climate change, supply chain pressures, and evolving consumer expectationsWhitney Mayer, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) Head of Global Sustainability, says the previous goals often targeted 2040 or 2050 and lacked the operational focus for immediate impact. The Source. Make. Delight. framework centers on three pillars: source strengthens resilient supply chains and cocoa sourcing for farmers; make pursues operational efficiency, waste reduction, and energy improvements; delight connects sustainability with consumer experience through transparent storytelling and data-driven decision-making to monitor progressBy aligning sustainability initiatives with business performance, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) aims to build a more resilient and efficient operation

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) EXPANDS BREAKFAST PORTFOLIO WITH CRUNCHY COOKIE CHOCOLATE SPREAD THROUGH HERSHEY INDIA

The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) through its subsidiary Hershey India Pvt Ltd. launched its Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread as part of its strategy to expand breakfast offerings in India, on 24th April 2026. The new Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread blends the rich flavor of creamy chocolate with the texture of crunchy cookies, targeting Millennials and Gen Z consumers who seek innovative and sensory experiences during breakfast. Hershey India's marketing campaign features the hashtag #TwoGoodTogether to highlight the product's appeal through relatable storytelling.Hershey India positions itself to promote togetherness, aligning with Indian cultural values that emphasize family and shared meals. By expanding its portfolio to include the Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread, Hershey India aims to enhance its existing offerings, which include variants of its popular chocolate spreads. Ankit Desai, Marketing Director at Hershey India, stated, "As a leading premium chocolate brand, we are committed to meeting the evolving needs of our consumers with innovative products. The launch of the \"Crunchy Cookie\" chocolate spread reflects our focus on delivering unique and enjoyable breakfast options for our customers."

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) LAUNCHES CONNECTED WORKER PROGRAM TO MODERNIZE MANUFACTURING

The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) launched its Connected Worker program on the 20th of April 2026, as part of its digital transformation in manufacturing, to empower factory teams with mobile tools for real-time insights, accelerated training, and improved communications across the production lineThe initiative replaces traditional paper checklists and manuals with digital tools. Operators access production data, training resources, and colleagues via mobile devices, ensuring knowledge is shared across shifts and plants. Generative AI helps create instructional content, and QR codes on equipment can surface relevant training materials instantlyMaintenance teams and supervisors gain clearer visibility into maintenance requests and performance metrics, enabling faster prioritization and more coaching time for leaders. Hershey aims to roll out the Connected Worker program to all manufacturing facilities, including salty snacks and confection factories, within 18 months

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ J.M. SMUCKER [3 News That Matters]

+ THE J.M. SMUCKER COMPANY (NYSE:SJM) COFFEE STRENGTH OUTPACES SNACK WEAKNESS IN INVESTMENT UPDATE

The J.M. Smucker Company (NYSE:SJM) faces a split trajectory as strong coffee results contrast with weakness in Sweet Baked Snacks, shaping a nuanced investment narrative. Wells Fargo analyst Chris Carey reaffirmed an Overweight rating while trimming sector estimates on inflation driven cost pressures and a cautious earnings outlook

+ THE J.M. SMUCKER COMPANY (NYSE:SJM) POSTS GROWTH ACROSS PET FOOD AND COFFEE AS CONSUMER TRENDS SHIFT

The J.M. Smucker Company (NYSE:SJM) stock is drawing investor interest as it leverages strength in pet food and coffee amid evolving consumer behavior and grocery dynamics. On 13th April 2026, investors weigh whether Smucker's Folgers, Jif, and Milk-Bone can sustain growth against competition and shifting preferences. The J.M. Smucker Company operates through three segments: U.S. Retail Coffee, U.S. Retail Consumer Foods, and U.S. Retail Pet Foods. The coffee segment remains the largest revenue contributor, while pet food is emerging as a key growth driver. The 2020 acquisition of Ainsworth Pet Nutrition strengthens high-quality pet foods amid rising pet humanization, and the Hostess Brands acquisition broadens the product mix. The company backs its strategy with a robust balance sheet and a history of dividend growth—27 consecutive years of increases. Yet challenges linger in the consumer foods space, notably declining peanut butter consumption amid allergy awareness. Looking ahead, quarterly results from pet foods and snacks will be pivotal to offset potential coffee-volume softness, while ongoing regulatory scrutiny and integration risks remain.

+ J.M. SMUCKER UNVEILS 30-YEAR PACKAGING REFRESH FOR SMUCKER’S FRUIT SPREADS

J.M. Smucker has introduced a packaging refresh for Smucker’s fruit spreads, the first major update in almost thirty years. The new jars began arriving in spring, from 6th April 2026, and they lean on a refreshed ’90s-inspired design with bolder colors, bigger fruit imagery, and a broader use of the gingham motif.The gingham pattern, long a staple on the jar lid, now appears on the front label as well. Each flavor will carry a distinct color to improve on-shelf identification, while the recipe and ingredients remain unchanged. Dayna Lewallen, senior design manager, creative and design at The J.M. Smucker Company, said, "This redesign was about honoring the Smucker’s brand’s most iconic assets and evolving them with modern intention."The refresh aligns with a broader trend of ’90s nostalgia in food and consumer culture. Smucker’s is using the redesign to modernize its shelf presence and help the spreads stand out in a category where visuals matter for trial, while signaling expanded usage occasions such as breakfast, snacking, charcuterie boards, and yogurt bowls.For retailers, the transition will roll out over the coming months with no reformulation or ingredient changes. The visual enhancements are designed to help consumers identify flavors quickly and give Smucker’s a more contemporary presence in the spreads aisle.Headquartered in Orrville, Ohio, J.M. Smucker leads major grocery categories including coffee, peanut butter and fruit spreads, with a portfolio that includes Folgers, Dunkin’, Cafe Bustelo, Jif, Uncrustables, Hostess, Milk-Bone and Meow Mix.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FERRERO INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ TOM BRADY LEADS FERRERO'S $100 MILLION WORLD CUP CAMPAIGN

Tom Brady has secured a partnership with Ferrero North America for the brand's World Cup campaign, announced on 30th March 2026. The collaboration places the seven-time Super Bowl champion at the center of Ferrero's 'Go All In' promotion, which marks the company's largest-ever marketing commitment at $100 million.The campaign targets North American audiences as the FIFA World Cup approaches, with Ten Toes playing a key role in identifying Brady and managing the collaboration, underscoring Ferrero's aim to bridge soccer's global appeal with U.S. culture.A commercial featuring Brady is slated to launch across streaming platforms, including Netflix, Amazon and HBO, as well as on social media channels, highlighting Ferrero's effort to reach domestic audiences.

+ FERRERO ANNOUNCES $1 MILLION GO ALL IN SOCCER PROMOTION WITH TOM BRADY

Ferrero International is launching its Go All In promotion tied to the global soccer tournament, featuring NFL legend Tom Brady. The program runs from Apr. 1, 2026 to Jul. 31, 2026 and offers entrants a grand prize of $1 million along with numerous daily and weekly prizes. Customers can enter by purchasing any two Ferrero products or opt for a free entry, making participation accessible to a wide audience. Brady's involvement adds star power as the campaign aims to energize fans across platforms. A 30-second advertisement featuring Brady will air starting Apr. 1, 2026 on Tubi, Netflix and YouTube, highlighting Ferrero's product lineup and the Go All In theme with the goal of driving engagement around soccer. Ferrero has earmarked about $100 million for the campaign, its largest marketing push to date, spanning traditional advertising, digital activations, influencer partnerships and in-store displays. The company will leverage beloved brands such as Nutella, Ferrero Rocher and Butterfinger to reach consumers in North America, supported by Ferrero North America president and chief business officer Michael Lindsey on turning casual shoppers into fans of the full Ferrero portfolio.

+ BARRY CALLEBAUT AND FERRERO LEAD UK COCOA COALITION TO ACCELERATE SUSTAINABLE SOURCING

Major chocolate manufacturers Barry Callebaut, Ferrero, The Hershey Company, and Tony’s Chocolonely have announced their membership in the UK Cocoa Coalition, a collective effort aimed at enhancing sustainability in cocoa supply chains. The coalition, which includes NGOs and retailers, is calling for the swift implementation of the UK Forest Risk Commodities Regulation to curb deforestation linked to cocoa sourcing.The formation comes amid concerns over environmental degradation and human rights violations within the cocoa industry. In a statement dated 26th March 2026, Ferrero UK’s Richard Laming stressed that collaboration is essential, noting that \"one single entity cannot address the challenges the cocoa industry is facing alone.\" The group has aligned with NGOs such as the Rainforest Alliance and the Fairtrade Foundation, and with retailers including Sainsbury’s and Marks & Spencer, signaling a shift toward agreed sustainability baselines rather than competition on ethics.Belinda Borck of Tony’s Chocolonely echoed the call for shared responsibility to address illegal deforestation and improve farmers' livelihoods. The coalition aims to drive policy that can enable systemic change, building a more resilient cocoa industry for farmers and the environment. The announcement marks a notable moment for the sector, as the UK remains a major market for chocolate confectionery.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PEPSICO [3 News That Matters]

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) SIGNS 10-YEAR VPPA WITH GIVAUDAN, SMURFIT WESTROCK AND STATKRAFT FOR SPAIN WIND ASSET

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) signed a 10-year Virtual Power Purchase Agreement (VPPA) with suppliers Givaudan, Smurfit WestRock, and Statkraft for a repowered wind asset in Spain on 28th April 2026 as part of its pep+ REnew program. The deal positions PepsiCo as the lead buyer to aggregate renewable electricity demand and strengthen the sustainability credentials of all parties involvedThe arrangement targets annual CO2 reductions of about 32,000 metric tons as the wind asset will be upgraded with more efficient turbines while leveraging existing substations and interconnection points. This marks the second cohort under pep+ REnew and the first renewable electricity cohort in Europe, reflecting the program launched in 2022 that has since supported more than 250 companies across North America, Latin America, Europe and Asia-PacificPepsiCo also reinforces its climate goals to cut Scope 3 Energy & Industry emissions by 42% and Scope 3 Forest, Land & Agriculture emissions by 30% by 2030, based on a 2022 baseline. Archana Jagannathan, Chief Sustainability Officer for PepsiCo Europe, Middle East, and Africa, notes that the agreement advances emissions reductions across the value chain. The initiative follows a 2023 PPA in Spain as the company continues to prioritize sustainability. On the financial side, PepsiCo reported nearly $94 billion in net revenue for 2025 and is trading around $154.10 with a P/E of 24.2, with a dividend yield of 3.69%. UBS has a Buy rating and a price target of $186, underscoring investor optimism about PepsiCo's growth prospects

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) TEAMS WITH GIVAUDAN, SMURFIT WESTROCK AND STATKRAFT ON A 10-YEAR VPPA FOR EUROPE RENEWABLE ENERGY

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) Teams With Givaudan, Smurfit WestRock and Statkraft on a 10-Year VPPA for Europe Renewable Energy. The parties finalize a 10-year Virtual Power Purchase Agreement on 28th of April, 2026 to back a wind energy asset in Spain that will undergo repowering under the pep+ REnew program. The arrangement pools energy demand across the three companies to access long-term renewable electricity opportunities typically reserved for larger firms, and marks PepsiCo's second power purchase in Spain. Under the pep+ REnew initiative, PepsiCo, SE Advisory Services and Schneider Electric structure the second supply-chain VPPA cohort, aligning the three companies to secure long-term renewable power. The wind project will be upgraded with more efficient turbines to boost output while minimizing environmental impact. PepsiCo expects to reduce about 32,000 metric tons of CO2 annually as a result of the arrangement. The company sets climate targets including a 42% reduction in Scope 3 Energy & Industry emissions and a 30% reduction in Scope 3 Forest, Land & Agriculture emissions by 2030, supporting a net-zero path by 2050 or sooner. Archana Jagannathan, Chief Sustainability Officer for PepsiCo Europe, Middle East and Africa, emphasizes collaboration to widen access to renewable energy, and Willem Mutsaerts of Givaudan highlights the tangible benefits of joint action. The wind repowering effort in Spain reinforces PepsiCo's second PPA in the country and its ongoing clean-energy push in Europe.

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) PARTNERS WITH CF INDUSTRIES TO DEPLOY LOW-CARBON UAN FERTILIZER IN POTATO SUPPLY CHAIN

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) has entered into a commercial agreement with CF Industries to reduce the carbon footprint of its potato supply chain. The partnership introduces a certified low-carbon urea ammonium nitrate (UAN) fertilizer, CF Industries’ first commercial launch of such a product, and it takes effect on 28th April 2026, reflecting a growing commitment to sustainable agriculture.The low-carbon UAN fertilizer is produced using carbon capture and sequestration technologies, alongside other emission-reduction methods, and is designed to lower greenhouse gas emissions that account for about 15-20 percent of emissions from potato farming. The fertilizer will be distributed to retailers and reach farmers who supply potatoes for PepsiCo’s Frito-Lay brands. Erik Mayer, Vice President of Clean Energy and Business Development at CF Industries, stated, "Our partnership with PepsiCo demonstrates the increasing demand for scalable solutions that can significantly reduce agricultural emissions without hindering productivity." Burgess Davis, Chief Sustainability Officer for North America at PepsiCo, added, "Collaborating with CF Industries allows us to integrate certified low-carbon fertilizers into our potato supply chain, thereby supporting farmers and contributing to a more sustainable agricultural framework." Looking ahead, CF Industries and PepsiCo have expressed intentions to explore additional opportunities for reducing emissions across various crop systems and to build a broader ecosystem around low-carbon fertilizers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Report: Carbon taxes cost Pennsylvania $5B

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGY-CMP3-RYY8-G4P1-00000-00Report: Carbon taxes cost Pennsylvania $5B2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:15ZLexisNexisNEWSReport: Carbon taxes cost Pennsylvania $5BChristen Smith The Center Square

(The Center Square) – Energy is big business in Pennsylvania.

Its storied fossil fuel reserves have kept utility bills lower and the regional power grid more stable for the better part of a century.

That’s remained key as states opt for lower-emission power sources, which have so far relied on fledgling technologies not yet able to keep the lights on with batteries, solar panels and wind turbines alone.

A new report from the Commonwealth Foundation concludes it could have been even bigger business had state regulators not spent the last six years “flirting” with a carbon tax program called the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative, the success of which many critics say relies on the fossil fuels it means to curb.

For the program’s most ardent supporters, the transition away from coal and gas must start somewhere. And, to them, a 50% reduction in emissions and $6 billion in revenue over the last 18 years isn’t a bad one.

“The Keystone State’s six-year on-again, off-again relationship with RGGI is a cautionary tale for other states, such as Virginia,” said Elizabeth Stelle, vice president of policy for the Commonwealth Foundation. “RGGI is a carbon tax in sheep’s clothing, toppling the energy future of every state it touches.”

RGGI, as the program is often referred to, profits from reductions in harmful emissions by charging generators for the air pollution they create, measured in carbon dioxide. Across 12 states in the mid-Atlantic and New England, power plants and industrial sites buy an ever-decreasing number of emissions “credits” at the program’s annual auction to cover that anticipated pollution.

Those credit prices have risen sharply since RGGI’s first auction in 2008, and because of the balance needed between fossil fuels and renewable power, the program has put financial pressure on the entire grid, leaving utility companies – and by extension, ratepayers – scrambling to make up the difference.

Sometimes relief comes from RGGI-funded bill-reduction programs administered by participating states. Or the proliferation of more efficient renewable sources, supported by government taxpayer-funded subsidies, that require less support from gas and coal. In recent years, it’s been the pooling of smart thermostats, rooftop solar panels, and battery storage to compensate consumers for lower electricity usage.

And yes, it’s been rate increases too. But if not for Pennsylvania, where 25% of the regional power grid’s electricity is sourced, conventional wisdom has long held that those hikes could have been far steeper.

Which is why former two-term Democratic Gov. Tom Wolf’s 2019 executive order adding Pennsylvania to the RGGI program triggered a six-year regulatory war with lawmakers to stop it before it ever happened.

The foundation, a conservative policy group based in Harrisburg, was among the loudest critics – behind legislative Republicans, with whom the organization typically aligns – of the proposal, citing quadrupled energy costs and a $5 billion investment shortfall, according to its report.

In terms of raw numbers, the chilling effect of potentially joining RGGI is stark. Between 2013 and 2018, 73% of proposed power projects in the commonwealth became operational. After regulators set their sights on RGGI in 2019, that conversion rate collapsed to 9%.

By comparison, the rate for Ohio – a non-RGGI state with a wealth of fossil fuel plants – declined much more slowly, from 62% to 48%, in part due to falling gas prices.

“Unfortunately, that loss is not shocking,” Stelle said. “While Pennsylvania debated, litigated, and delayed over carbon tax policy, Ohio provided developers with a predictable regulatory environment to commit billions in capital. Projects that might have started in Pennsylvania went to Ohio or never came to fruition.”

Losing to “friggin’ Ohio” has jokingly been a thorn in the side of first-term Democratic Gov. Josh Shapiro since he took office in 2023, even calling out the Buckeye State’s competitive economic policies during his first budget address to the General Assembly.

Shapiro, on the campaign trail, expressed his doubts about RGGI, though he continued the Wolf administration’s legal fight to make Pennsylvania participate for two more years, only dropping it as part of a four-month-late budget deal this past November.

RGGI detractors say taxing generators would not just make it less profitable to build new power plants. It would raise energy costs for everyone by accelerating the closure of long-serving facilities, reducing supply, and raising electricity prices.

Shapiro, too, worries about affordability and instability, though he’s pointed the finger at PJM, the organization that manages the power grid, and at its lengthy planning process. He even filed complaints with federal regulators to force the organization to lower electricity price caps, temporarily preventing a 30% spike for ratepayers.

He’s even threatened, more than once, to decouple the state from the grid and “go it alone” – an unprecedented move that could undo a century of cooperation.

It hasn’t come to that yet. Instead, Shapiro has lobbied the General Assembly to act on his state-centric Lightning Plan to quadruple wind and solar power over the decade – and still limit emissions from larger plants.

Stelle said doing so would double residential electricity bills.

“Affordable, reliable energy is not achieved through Green New Deal-style mandates and burdensome regulation,” she said. “It happens naturally when we pursue policies that welcome the investment its grid desperately needs, letting Pennsylvania’s rich energy sector do what it does best.”

In the eyes of some, air pollution is what the energy industry does best. In 2020, federal data rankedPennsylvania fourth nationwide for carbon emissions, and estimates suggest that 1% of global greenhouse gas pollution comes from the commonwealth alone.

Supporters of programs like RGGI often point to the dual impacts of reduced emissions and investment in renewable technology as key to their value for an energy-rich state like Pennsylvania.

A 2023 report from the Kleinman Center for Energy Policy at the University of Pennsylvania concluded that RGGI participation would have reduced the state’s electricity-sector emissions by 84% in 2030, compared to roughly 52% without it.

It could have been achieved without raising utility bills or decimating energy exports, according to the report. Researchers said wind and solar generation would have softened the impact of lost natural gas production and retail electricity prices will actually decline 0.6% by the time the RGGI cap zeroes out in 2040.

Shapiro’s state-based plans – the Pennsylvania Reliable Energy Sustainability Standard and the Pennsylvania Climate Emissions Reduction Act, PRESS and PACER for short – similarly broaden the mix of energy providers so that the power grid isn’t sustained primarily by fossil fuels.

This time, however, 70% of the money recovered from state-imposed emissions caps would support bill-reduction programs.

And, based on modeling from Synapse Energy Economics that was commissioned by the state Department of Environmental Protection last year, it would lower residential bills by $1 per month.

What’s not entirely clear is how rapid data center development would collide with such policy, if it were to be implemented. More than 50 proposals are pending across the state, and their economic viability could falter if the state recommits to carbon taxing programs.

Steam plumes rise from a geothermal power plant. Photo by Sam Bark/Unsplash

value.Title: MARS, INCORPORATED - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-VC63-RX36-R3HD-00000-00MARS, INCORPORATED - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisSTOCKMARS, INCORPORATED - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: MARS, INCORPORATED

PermID5040171391Websitehttp://www.mars.comIndustryPackaged Foods & Confectionery

ACTIVITIES:

Mars, Incorporated, founded in 1911, is a family-owned global confectionery and pet care company headquartered in McLean, Virginia. Employing over 140,000 people across 80 countries, it generates over $45 billion annually. Known for brands like M&Ms, Snickers, and Pedigree, Mars operates in confectionery, pet care, and food. In 2024, it acquired Kellanova for $35.9 billion, expanding its snack portfolio. Its culture, guided by Five Principles (Quality, Responsibility, Mutuality, Efficiency, Freedom), emphasizes sustainability and employee well-being. Competing with Nestl, Mars stands out for its private ownership and sustainability goals, targeting net-zero emissions by 2050. Recent initiatives include investments in plant-based foods and digital supply chain solutions.

+ MARS, INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ MARS SECURES MAJOR CLEAN ENERGY PACT IN LITHUANIA

On April 28, 2026, Mars, Incorporated announced a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for the majority output from the Skuodas Wind Farm in Lithuania. The deal is expected to generate about 490 GWh of renewable electricity annually and reduce carbon emissions by approximately 120,000 tons per year. The Skuodas Wind Farm has an installed capacity of 158.4 MW and will supply renewable energy to Mars' consumption and value chain, supporting the Renewables Acceleration Program and the company's net-zero ambitions.Kevin Rabinovitch, Global VP of Sustainability at Mars, called the arrangement a milestone for real-world climate action, noting that "this agreement with European Energy helps bring new wind power online in Lithuania and strengthens our ability to extend credible renewable electricity across our value chain." The project is scheduled to go live in 2028 and will reinforce Mars' Lithuania manufacturing facility with a reliable source of renewable electricity, while also expanding domestic wind capacity in Europe through the corporate PPA.

+ MARS INC BROADENS RENEWABLE FOOTPRINT WITH LITHUANIAN WIND PPA

Mars Inc has signed a wind power purchase agreement to power its Lithuanian pet food factory, signaling a firm step toward sustainable operations. The PPA was finalized on 28th April 2026, as Mars moves to shift its Lithuanian operations to greener energy and aligns with its plan to reach net zero greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2050.By tapping wind energy, Mars aims to reduce emissions and set an example for the pet food sector. Lithuania has been expanding renewable capacity, reaching about 6 GW of combined wind and solar generation, and the wind PPA comes as European firms boost renewable investments.The Lithuanian plant is a key link in Mars European supply chain, producing popular pet nutrition brands. The deal is expected to improve ecological efficiency and could attract investors who value sustainability, while signaling Mars leadership in responsible manufacturing.

+ MARS LAUNCHES PROTECT THE PEANUT CAMPAIGN TO SAFEGUARD M&M'S PEANUT QUALITY

Mars Inc. has rolled out a global Protect the Peanut campaign for M&M's across social channels, kicking off on 7th April 2026, and featuring a short documentary by BBDO Chicago that addresses consumer concerns about Peanut M&M's and points to peanut quality rather than candy flaws. Peanuts are notoriously vulnerable to disease and weather, with roughly 30% of crops failing before harvest. For M&M's, only about 1 in 100 peanuts meets their exacting quality standard. Mars says the effort blends securing the crop's future with launching a hardier peanut variety, backed by roughly $10 million invested over a decade in research with scientists from the University of Georgia. The initiative also includes additional funding—about $12 million in premiums—to help farmers transition to the new peanut type. Early indicators suggest Peanut M&M's complaints have fallen by around 90%. As BBDO’s Josh Gross put it, "Sometimes the most powerful ideas start with someone noticing something small and then starting to ask the right questions," underscoring the campaign's aim to address core issues and protect a beloved product.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) LAUNCHES SNACK NA SWAK INITIATIVE TO PROMOTE MINDFUL FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondelez International Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) launched its Snack na Swak initiative on Apr. 27, 2026, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos by encouraging mindful choices within a recommended 250-calorie range advised by local health authorities. The campaign treats snacking as a cultural touchpoint, from quick merienda between meetings to shared family moments, and promotes mindful snacking as an opportunity to balance portions, nutrition, and the overall experience. An interactive session at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist Jo Sebastian guiding participants through the Portion Puzzle, stressing the importance of nutrition labels for confident choices. Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, called for clearer labeling and more portion-controlled options to aid healthier decisions. Mondelez International also notes its role in shaping consumption patterns and has introduced portion-controlled products to improve access to healthier options, reframing familiar snacks such as Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VICE PRESIDENT – MARKETING

On 22nd April 2026, Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing, with Viswanathan having spent nearly eight years at Mondelez and most recently serving as Senior Director of Marketing. In his new role, he will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, including brand equity, innovation, and activation, and will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with India and Global Technology Leadership. Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008, later serving as VP Marketing – Chocolates at Cadbury Kraft India from 2011 to 2013. He joined Mondelez International in 2014, relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, and returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020. The promotion comes as Mondelez International focuses on strengthening its market position in India amid a digital-forward consumer landscape and ongoing emphasis on innovative marketing strategies

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL, INC. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) GETS UPGRADED BY BOFA ON POSITIVE MANAGEMENT TALKS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) attracted investor attention after Bank of America raised its price target on the snack maker to $65 from $62 and kept a Buy rating, citing positive management updates from meetings in March. On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its constructive stance and flagged ongoing discussions with European chocolate retailers as a potential growth avenueUBS trimmed its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 and maintained a Neutral rating, with the bank expecting first-quarter results across consumer staples to show some improvement while warning that inflation could weigh on earnings in the second half of 2026Mondelez, which produces chocolate, biscuits and baked snacks, also has exposure to gum, candy, cheese, grocery items and powdered beverages. The stock is among 13 NASDAQ names with the highest dividends, adding a yield-oriented angle for investors evaluating the name

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER PLC (LSE:ULVR) WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) is weighing a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to sharpen growth in its beauty, personal care and wellbeing brands. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions said the group is engaging advisers to assess options, including a potential divestment of the food business, which includes Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes. Deliberations are in early stages, with scenarios ranging from a full spin-off to retaining flagship brands while divesting others; a formal decision is not expected before 2027. Industry insiders peg a deal value in the tens of billions of dollars if pursued, though no path has been finalized. Unilever's food arm includes Colman's condiments and Marmite, and the stock has traded about 0.7% lower, placing the company's market capitalization around £107 billion ($142 billion). Under CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is refocusing on its beauty and wellbeing brands, having spun off the ice cream division last year while retaining a minority stake to be sold gradually. Analysts such as Warren Ackerman at Barclays note the direction toward beauty is clear, but timing for a food separation may be sensitive given market conditions. The group has previously divested the global spreads division and snack brands, leaving roughly €1 billion to €1.5 billion of local food brands to exit

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) AI-DRIVEN INNOVATION ACCELERATES BEAUTY AND WELLBEING DEVELOPMENT

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) On 28th April 2026, the company disclosed that it is leveraging artificial intelligence to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling products to be created in days rather than months. Its €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is deploying AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, drawing from more than 1,000 external data sources each month to guide product development. Unique AI insights tools synthesize findings with the company's extensive R&D databases, streamlining the formulation process and reducing development cycles from five or six rounds to one or two. The time to move from concept to market has shrunk from months to days, and the claims generation process is 75% quicker, boosting overall efficiency.Beyond faster formulation, the company taps its R&D datasets to explore biological data and run complex research at unprecedented speeds. The introduction of virtual cohorts enables scientists to simulate demographic responses to new products, cutting time and costs for early-stage testing without forgoing real-world trials. AI-driven innovations have already led to launches such as the Hydra Miracle range, which uses a groundbreaking ingredient to enhance skin hydration. The Damage Therapy range is delivering double-digit growth, underscoring how AI supports both efficiency and scientific discovery. As the Chief R&D Officer notes, structured data alongside human creativity is redefining the possibilities in research and development.

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) RECEIVES MCCORMICK OFFER VALUED AT €29 BILLION

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) receives an offer from McCormick & Co. for its food division, valued at about €29 billion ($33 billion). The parties disclosed the approach on Apr. 26, 2026, with Unilever confirming discussions are underway and that terms remain undisclosed. The talks signal the potential reshaping of both groups, though no binding agreement has been reached

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS, INC. (NYSE:GIS) EXPANDS OMNICHANNEL PROGRAM TO STRENGTHEN GROWTH TRAJECTORY

General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) is positioning omnichannel execution as a central pillar of its Remarkable Experience framework, underscoring its role in driving more consistent consumer engagement across digital and physical retail environments. Apr. 14, 2026 marked a demonstration that its focus on online and offline integration is delivering tangible results.A primary driver has been General Mills' ability to leverage its portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to achieve differential retail execution. The company has ramped up investments in retail media to capture consumers who prioritize convenience alongside value. This targeted approach to winning online has yielded a tripling of its e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners. The omnichannel momentum is corroborated by seven of the top ten North America retail categories showing rising household penetration through the fiscal third quarter.

+ GENERAL MILLS INC. (NYSE:GIS) POSTS €1.7 BILLION JUNIOR SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) Posts €1.7 Billion in Junior Subordinated Notes Offering to Strengthen Capital Flexibility. The €1.7 billion issue comprises €1.0 billion of Series A notes at 4.750% fixed until 16 July 2031, after which the rate resets to the Five-Year Swap Rate plus 202.4 basis points, and €700 million of Series B notes at 5.250% fixed until 16 July 2034, with resets tied to the same swap rate plus 239.0 basis points. Both series use fixed-to-fixed rate structures designed to provide a predictable income stream for investors while giving General Mills flexibility in financing plans. The offering was made public on 14th April 2026. Interest payments are scheduled annually on 16th July, beginning in 2026. The notes include step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin five years after the first reset date, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset date. General Mills also reserves the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years, with any deferred interest accruing at the applicable rate. The company may redeem the notes at any time under the terms of the prospectus supplement. Classified as junior subordinated debt, the notes carry a lower claim on assets in liquidation but provide a strategic means to strengthen capital structure and support growth initiatives.

+ GENERAL MILLS REFRESHES CEREAL LINE TO ALIGN WITH CONSUMER TRENDS

General Mills on 7th April 2026 rolled out two cereal innovations, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney's Moana, part of a broader cereal refresh that aims to remove artificial colors and broaden flavor options through brand collaborations.General Mills traded at 37.67 dollars, about 7.9 percent below the consensus target of 40.89, with recent results showing weakness in the stock, including a 14.9 percent drop over the last 30 days and a 51.2 percent decline over three years. The cereal launches are described as part of a longer term strategy to rebuild brand relevance and consumer engagement, even as earnings are forecast to fall about 6.3 percent annually. The company also confronts valuation questions, as shares sit well below estimated fair value with a price-to-earnings ratio near 9.1 and debt levels that raise questions about cash-flow sufficiency.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HERSHEY COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) SETS SOURCE. MAKE. DELIGHT. SUSTAINABILITY FRAMEWORK

On 27th April 2026, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) Sets its new Source. Make. Delight. framework to embed sustainability into its core operations rather than treating it as an ancillary task, a response to climate change, supply chain pressures, and evolving consumer expectationsWhitney Mayer, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) Head of Global Sustainability, says the previous goals often targeted 2040 or 2050 and lacked the operational focus for immediate impact. The Source. Make. Delight. framework centers on three pillars: source strengthens resilient supply chains and cocoa sourcing for farmers; make pursues operational efficiency, waste reduction, and energy improvements; delight connects sustainability with consumer experience through transparent storytelling and data-driven decision-making to monitor progressBy aligning sustainability initiatives with business performance, The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) aims to build a more resilient and efficient operation

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) EXPANDS BREAKFAST PORTFOLIO WITH CRUNCHY COOKIE CHOCOLATE SPREAD THROUGH HERSHEY INDIA

The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) through its subsidiary Hershey India Pvt Ltd. launched its Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread as part of its strategy to expand breakfast offerings in India, on 24th April 2026. The new Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread blends the rich flavor of creamy chocolate with the texture of crunchy cookies, targeting Millennials and Gen Z consumers who seek innovative and sensory experiences during breakfast. Hershey India's marketing campaign features the hashtag #TwoGoodTogether to highlight the product's appeal through relatable storytelling.Hershey India positions itself to promote togetherness, aligning with Indian cultural values that emphasize family and shared meals. By expanding its portfolio to include the Crunchy Cookie chocolate spread, Hershey India aims to enhance its existing offerings, which include variants of its popular chocolate spreads. Ankit Desai, Marketing Director at Hershey India, stated, "As a leading premium chocolate brand, we are committed to meeting the evolving needs of our consumers with innovative products. The launch of the \"Crunchy Cookie\" chocolate spread reflects our focus on delivering unique and enjoyable breakfast options for our customers."

+ THE HERSHEY COMPANY (NYSE:HSY) LAUNCHES CONNECTED WORKER PROGRAM TO MODERNIZE MANUFACTURING

The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) launched its Connected Worker program on the 20th of April 2026, as part of its digital transformation in manufacturing, to empower factory teams with mobile tools for real-time insights, accelerated training, and improved communications across the production lineThe initiative replaces traditional paper checklists and manuals with digital tools. Operators access production data, training resources, and colleagues via mobile devices, ensuring knowledge is shared across shifts and plants. Generative AI helps create instructional content, and QR codes on equipment can surface relevant training materials instantlyMaintenance teams and supervisors gain clearer visibility into maintenance requests and performance metrics, enabling faster prioritization and more coaching time for leaders. Hershey aims to roll out the Connected Worker program to all manufacturing facilities, including salty snacks and confection factories, within 18 months

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ J.M. SMUCKER [3 News That Matters]

+ THE J.M. SMUCKER COMPANY (NYSE:SJM) COFFEE STRENGTH OUTPACES SNACK WEAKNESS IN INVESTMENT UPDATE

The J.M. Smucker Company (NYSE:SJM) faces a split trajectory as strong coffee results contrast with weakness in Sweet Baked Snacks, shaping a nuanced investment narrative. Wells Fargo analyst Chris Carey reaffirmed an Overweight rating while trimming sector estimates on inflation driven cost pressures and a cautious earnings outlook

+ THE J.M. SMUCKER COMPANY (NYSE:SJM) POSTS GROWTH ACROSS PET FOOD AND COFFEE AS CONSUMER TRENDS SHIFT

The J.M. Smucker Company (NYSE:SJM) stock is drawing investor interest as it leverages strength in pet food and coffee amid evolving consumer behavior and grocery dynamics. On 13th April 2026, investors weigh whether Smucker's Folgers, Jif, and Milk-Bone can sustain growth against competition and shifting preferences. The J.M. Smucker Company operates through three segments: U.S. Retail Coffee, U.S. Retail Consumer Foods, and U.S. Retail Pet Foods. The coffee segment remains the largest revenue contributor, while pet food is emerging as a key growth driver. The 2020 acquisition of Ainsworth Pet Nutrition strengthens high-quality pet foods amid rising pet humanization, and the Hostess Brands acquisition broadens the product mix. The company backs its strategy with a robust balance sheet and a history of dividend growth—27 consecutive years of increases. Yet challenges linger in the consumer foods space, notably declining peanut butter consumption amid allergy awareness. Looking ahead, quarterly results from pet foods and snacks will be pivotal to offset potential coffee-volume softness, while ongoing regulatory scrutiny and integration risks remain.

+ J.M. SMUCKER UNVEILS 30-YEAR PACKAGING REFRESH FOR SMUCKER’S FRUIT SPREADS

J.M. Smucker has introduced a packaging refresh for Smucker’s fruit spreads, the first major update in almost thirty years. The new jars began arriving in spring, from 6th April 2026, and they lean on a refreshed ’90s-inspired design with bolder colors, bigger fruit imagery, and a broader use of the gingham motif.The gingham pattern, long a staple on the jar lid, now appears on the front label as well. Each flavor will carry a distinct color to improve on-shelf identification, while the recipe and ingredients remain unchanged. Dayna Lewallen, senior design manager, creative and design at The J.M. Smucker Company, said, "This redesign was about honoring the Smucker’s brand’s most iconic assets and evolving them with modern intention."The refresh aligns with a broader trend of ’90s nostalgia in food and consumer culture. Smucker’s is using the redesign to modernize its shelf presence and help the spreads stand out in a category where visuals matter for trial, while signaling expanded usage occasions such as breakfast, snacking, charcuterie boards, and yogurt bowls.For retailers, the transition will roll out over the coming months with no reformulation or ingredient changes. The visual enhancements are designed to help consumers identify flavors quickly and give Smucker’s a more contemporary presence in the spreads aisle.Headquartered in Orrville, Ohio, J.M. Smucker leads major grocery categories including coffee, peanut butter and fruit spreads, with a portfolio that includes Folgers, Dunkin’, Cafe Bustelo, Jif, Uncrustables, Hostess, Milk-Bone and Meow Mix.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FERRERO INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ TOM BRADY LEADS FERRERO'S $100 MILLION WORLD CUP CAMPAIGN

Tom Brady has secured a partnership with Ferrero North America for the brand's World Cup campaign, announced on 30th March 2026. The collaboration places the seven-time Super Bowl champion at the center of Ferrero's 'Go All In' promotion, which marks the company's largest-ever marketing commitment at $100 million.The campaign targets North American audiences as the FIFA World Cup approaches, with Ten Toes playing a key role in identifying Brady and managing the collaboration, underscoring Ferrero's aim to bridge soccer's global appeal with U.S. culture.A commercial featuring Brady is slated to launch across streaming platforms, including Netflix, Amazon and HBO, as well as on social media channels, highlighting Ferrero's effort to reach domestic audiences.

+ FERRERO ANNOUNCES $1 MILLION GO ALL IN SOCCER PROMOTION WITH TOM BRADY

Ferrero International is launching its Go All In promotion tied to the global soccer tournament, featuring NFL legend Tom Brady. The program runs from Apr. 1, 2026 to Jul. 31, 2026 and offers entrants a grand prize of $1 million along with numerous daily and weekly prizes. Customers can enter by purchasing any two Ferrero products or opt for a free entry, making participation accessible to a wide audience. Brady's involvement adds star power as the campaign aims to energize fans across platforms. A 30-second advertisement featuring Brady will air starting Apr. 1, 2026 on Tubi, Netflix and YouTube, highlighting Ferrero's product lineup and the Go All In theme with the goal of driving engagement around soccer. Ferrero has earmarked about $100 million for the campaign, its largest marketing push to date, spanning traditional advertising, digital activations, influencer partnerships and in-store displays. The company will leverage beloved brands such as Nutella, Ferrero Rocher and Butterfinger to reach consumers in North America, supported by Ferrero North America president and chief business officer Michael Lindsey on turning casual shoppers into fans of the full Ferrero portfolio.

+ BARRY CALLEBAUT AND FERRERO LEAD UK COCOA COALITION TO ACCELERATE SUSTAINABLE SOURCING

Major chocolate manufacturers Barry Callebaut, Ferrero, The Hershey Company, and Tony’s Chocolonely have announced their membership in the UK Cocoa Coalition, a collective effort aimed at enhancing sustainability in cocoa supply chains. The coalition, which includes NGOs and retailers, is calling for the swift implementation of the UK Forest Risk Commodities Regulation to curb deforestation linked to cocoa sourcing.The formation comes amid concerns over environmental degradation and human rights violations within the cocoa industry. In a statement dated 26th March 2026, Ferrero UK’s Richard Laming stressed that collaboration is essential, noting that \"one single entity cannot address the challenges the cocoa industry is facing alone.\" The group has aligned with NGOs such as the Rainforest Alliance and the Fairtrade Foundation, and with retailers including Sainsbury’s and Marks & Spencer, signaling a shift toward agreed sustainability baselines rather than competition on ethics.Belinda Borck of Tony’s Chocolonely echoed the call for shared responsibility to address illegal deforestation and improve farmers' livelihoods. The coalition aims to drive policy that can enable systemic change, building a more resilient cocoa industry for farmers and the environment. The announcement marks a notable moment for the sector, as the UK remains a major market for chocolate confectionery.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PEPSICO [3 News That Matters]

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) SIGNS 10-YEAR VPPA WITH GIVAUDAN, SMURFIT WESTROCK AND STATKRAFT FOR SPAIN WIND ASSET

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) signed a 10-year Virtual Power Purchase Agreement (VPPA) with suppliers Givaudan, Smurfit WestRock, and Statkraft for a repowered wind asset in Spain on 28th April 2026 as part of its pep+ REnew program. The deal positions PepsiCo as the lead buyer to aggregate renewable electricity demand and strengthen the sustainability credentials of all parties involvedThe arrangement targets annual CO2 reductions of about 32,000 metric tons as the wind asset will be upgraded with more efficient turbines while leveraging existing substations and interconnection points. This marks the second cohort under pep+ REnew and the first renewable electricity cohort in Europe, reflecting the program launched in 2022 that has since supported more than 250 companies across North America, Latin America, Europe and Asia-PacificPepsiCo also reinforces its climate goals to cut Scope 3 Energy & Industry emissions by 42% and Scope 3 Forest, Land & Agriculture emissions by 30% by 2030, based on a 2022 baseline. Archana Jagannathan, Chief Sustainability Officer for PepsiCo Europe, Middle East, and Africa, notes that the agreement advances emissions reductions across the value chain. The initiative follows a 2023 PPA in Spain as the company continues to prioritize sustainability. On the financial side, PepsiCo reported nearly $94 billion in net revenue for 2025 and is trading around $154.10 with a P/E of 24.2, with a dividend yield of 3.69%. UBS has a Buy rating and a price target of $186, underscoring investor optimism about PepsiCo's growth prospects

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) TEAMS WITH GIVAUDAN, SMURFIT WESTROCK AND STATKRAFT ON A 10-YEAR VPPA FOR EUROPE RENEWABLE ENERGY

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) Teams With Givaudan, Smurfit WestRock and Statkraft on a 10-Year VPPA for Europe Renewable Energy. The parties finalize a 10-year Virtual Power Purchase Agreement on 28th of April, 2026 to back a wind energy asset in Spain that will undergo repowering under the pep+ REnew program. The arrangement pools energy demand across the three companies to access long-term renewable electricity opportunities typically reserved for larger firms, and marks PepsiCo's second power purchase in Spain. Under the pep+ REnew initiative, PepsiCo, SE Advisory Services and Schneider Electric structure the second supply-chain VPPA cohort, aligning the three companies to secure long-term renewable power. The wind project will be upgraded with more efficient turbines to boost output while minimizing environmental impact. PepsiCo expects to reduce about 32,000 metric tons of CO2 annually as a result of the arrangement. The company sets climate targets including a 42% reduction in Scope 3 Energy & Industry emissions and a 30% reduction in Scope 3 Forest, Land & Agriculture emissions by 2030, supporting a net-zero path by 2050 or sooner. Archana Jagannathan, Chief Sustainability Officer for PepsiCo Europe, Middle East and Africa, emphasizes collaboration to widen access to renewable energy, and Willem Mutsaerts of Givaudan highlights the tangible benefits of joint action. The wind repowering effort in Spain reinforces PepsiCo's second PPA in the country and its ongoing clean-energy push in Europe.

+ PEPSICO, INC. (NASDAQ:PEP) PARTNERS WITH CF INDUSTRIES TO DEPLOY LOW-CARBON UAN FERTILIZER IN POTATO SUPPLY CHAIN

PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) has entered into a commercial agreement with CF Industries to reduce the carbon footprint of its potato supply chain. The partnership introduces a certified low-carbon urea ammonium nitrate (UAN) fertilizer, CF Industries’ first commercial launch of such a product, and it takes effect on 28th April 2026, reflecting a growing commitment to sustainable agriculture.The low-carbon UAN fertilizer is produced using carbon capture and sequestration technologies, alongside other emission-reduction methods, and is designed to lower greenhouse gas emissions that account for about 15-20 percent of emissions from potato farming. The fertilizer will be distributed to retailers and reach farmers who supply potatoes for PepsiCo’s Frito-Lay brands. Erik Mayer, Vice President of Clean Energy and Business Development at CF Industries, stated, "Our partnership with PepsiCo demonstrates the increasing demand for scalable solutions that can significantly reduce agricultural emissions without hindering productivity." Burgess Davis, Chief Sustainability Officer for North America at PepsiCo, added, "Collaborating with CF Industries allows us to integrate certified low-carbon fertilizers into our potato supply chain, thereby supporting farmers and contributing to a more sustainable agricultural framework." Looking ahead, CF Industries and PepsiCo have expressed intentions to explore additional opportunities for reducing emissions across various crop systems and to build a broader ecosystem around low-carbon fertilizers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: No Big Fossil Bailouts on Your Power Bill Act Legislation by Sen. Markey Analyzed

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-T4S3-RX1F-W0W3-00000-00No Big Fossil Bailouts on Your Power Bill Act Legislation by Sen. Markey Analyzed2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:44ZLexisNexisNo Big Fossil Bailouts on Your Power Bill Act Legislation by Sen. Markey AnalyzedBailey Malota, Targeted News Service

WASHINGTON, May 1 -- The No Big Fossil Bailouts on Your Power Bill Act, originally introduced by Sen. Edward J. Markey, D-MA, on April 16, 2026, has been analyzed by the Congressional Research Service. This legislation aims to limit emergency powers granted under the Federal Power Act, specifically concerning the operation of fossil fuel-powered electric generating facilities.

The primary motivation behind this bill stems from growing concerns about climate change and the financial burdens of keeping outdated fossil fuel plants operational. By restricting the use of emergency authority that can allow these facilities to remain online, the legislation seeks to encourage a transition toward cleaner energy alternatives. The proposed amendments to the Federal Power Act mandate that when emergency measures are considered, utility commissions must evaluate environmentally sustainable options and assess potential cost impacts on consumers.

This bill is particularly timely as the nation grapples with energy crises exacerbated by extreme weather events and global supply chain disruptions. The legislation emphasizes accountability by requiring public hearings and ensuring that consumers are informed about how emergency orders could affect both rates and environmental compliance. By establishing a thorough review process for such emergency decisions, the bill aims to prioritize renewable energy and infrastructure that promotes sustainability over short-term fossil fuel reliance.

Critics argue that the bill could hinder necessary emergency actions during critical moments, but supporters believe that its rigorous framework safeguards both consumers and the environment. This legislation exemplifies a growing legislative trend focused on alleviating the dependency on fossil fuels while fostering a clean energy future. As climate-related challenges intensify, the implications of this bill could play a significant role in shaping the United States' energy landscape in the years to come.

The bill (S. 4337) has 4 co-sponsors: Sens. Peter Welch, D-VT; Chris Van Hollen, D-MD; Jeff Merkley, D-OR; Lisa Blunt Rochester, D-DE.

value.Title: Negating Neighborhood Noise Act Legislation by Rep. Williams Analyzed

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK9-6YK3-RSX4-R561-00000-00Negating Neighborhood Noise Act Legislation by Rep. Williams Analyzed2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisNegating Neighborhood Noise Act Legislation by Rep. Williams AnalyzedBailey Malota, Targeted News Service

WASHINGTON, May 14 -- The Negating Neighborhood Noise Act, originally introduced by Rep. Nikema Williams, D-GA, on May 7, 2026, has been analyzed by the Congressional Research Service. The bill seeks to amend the National Highway System Designation Act of 1995 to allow for the construction of specific noise barriers using funds from the Highway Trust Fund, thereby addressing the community's need for noise mitigation along major highways.

This legislation arises from growing concerns over rising noise pollution in residential areas adjacent to highways. As urban populations continue to expand, many communities experience significant disturbances from traffic noise, which can adversely affect residents' quality of life, health, and property values. By enabling the construction of Type II noise barriers-designed to mitigate highway noise-the bill aims to improve living conditions for affected populations.

One noteworthy feature of the bill is its provision for multipurpose noise barriers. The Secretary of Transportation will oversee that these barriers not only reduce noise but also have secondary beneficial uses, such as hosting renewable energy generation systems or broadband infrastructure. This aspect reflects a creative approach to maximize the utility of infrastructure investments, potentially leading to enhanced economic and environmental outcomes.

Furthermore, the bill emphasizes aesthetics in the design of new noise barriers, requiring compliance with guidelines from the Federal Highway Administration's Noise Barrier Design Handbook. This focus on visual impact ensures that noise mitigation solutions are not only functional but also considerate of community aesthetics.

As discussions continue around urban planning and environmental standards, the Negating Neighborhood Noise Act represents a significant step toward addressing the challenges posed by noise pollution while fostering innovation in infrastructure development.

The bill (H.R. 8717) has 3 co-sponsors: Reps. James A. Himes, D-CT; Eleanor Holmes Norton, D-DC; Sean Casten, D-IL.

* * # * *

Primary source of information: https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/8717/text
