value.Title: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-WBY3-S09D-H563-00000-00NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:49ZLexisNexisSTOCKNANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY

PermID4296599588Websitehttps://www.ntu.edu.sg/Pages/home.aspxIndustryUniversityAddress50 Nanyang Avenue, Singapore 639798

ACTIVITIES:

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), founded in 1991, is a public research university in Singapore. With over 7,000 employees and 33,000 students, it generates approximately $1.5 billion in annual revenue. Renowned for engineering, business, and sciences, NTU ranks among Asias top universities, excelling in AI, robotics, and sustainability research. Its campus hosts the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine and cutting-edge facilities like the Smart Campus initiative. In 2024, NTU expanded its green energy research, focusing on hydrogen technologies. Competing with the National University of Singapore, NTUs mission is to drive innovation and global impact through interdisciplinary education and transformative research, fostering industry partnerships and societal contributions.

+ NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YAP WEE KWONG’S GIFT AT NTU AIMS TO BROADEN ACCESS FOR STUDENTS FACING FINANCIAL HURDLES

NTU alumnus Yap Wee Kwong, who studied Accountancy and became the first in his family to graduate in 1996, has funded a new bursary after his daughter inspired him. On 11 May 2026, he spoke about his journey and his hope to open opportunities for the next generation.During his student days, financial aid was limited; he relied on student loans and part-time work while his father drove a bus and his mother stayed at home. He said, "My family background wasn't that strong. What I wanted to do was to graduate so I could work and earn money."After earning a second degree in 2005, he returned to NTU in 2025 as a parent with his daughter Lynnette, who is studying Environmental Sciences and Data Analytics. He observed the campus's transformation and the broader choices now available, noting that today "Nowadays students can pursue second majors or even double degrees." The philanthropic journey started with modest contributions, then his daughter's opportunities prompted him and wife Chan Seow Wai to pledge S$150,000 in June 2025 to establish the Yap Wee Kwong & Family Gratitude Bursary, to support financially needy undergraduates, particularly in the Asian School of the Environment at NTU. The bursary aims to help students with potential who face financial obstacles, and he hopes it inspires future generations to give back. "If we can contribute, we should try to do so as much as we can," he said.

+ NTU CHEMIST ZHAO YANLI EARNS AIMBE FELLOWSHIP FOR ADVANCES IN BIOIMAGING AND NANOMEDICINE

NTU Singapore today announced that Professor Zhao Yanli of the School of Chemistry, Chemical Engineering and Biotechnology has been elected a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering (AIMBE). The induction took place on April 13, 2026, at the AIMBE Annual Event in Arlington, Virginia, USA.Zhao is recognized for pioneering interdisciplinary work in materials chemistry and biomedical engineering that advances bioimaging, therapy and nanomedicine platforms. His contributions place him among the top two percent of medical and biological engineers, and the AIMBE College of Fellows is regarded as one of the highest professional distinctions in these fields.At NTU, Zhao leads a research group focused on developing integrated functional systems for targeted bioimaging and therapy in disease treatment. The team is pursuing approaches that combine imaging capabilities with therapeutic delivery, enabling clinicians to visualize diseased tissue while administering drugs.The group has recently developed single-metal atom biocatalysts designed for applications such as antitumor immunotherapy. These catalysts are characterized by high catalytic activity and selectivity, aided by well defined structures and tailored designs for the microenvironments of different diseases.Researchers are studying the atomic structure of these biocatalysts to optimize catalytic active sites and to better understand the mechanisms behind their biocatalytic processes. Zhao says the election underscores the groundbreaking work taking place at NTU and reinforces the university commitment to advancing science and technology and to inspiring future innovations.

+ NTU ADVANCES MATERIALS RESEARCH VIA NTU-AI4X POSTDOCTORAL APPOINTMENTS

NTU has expanded its NTU-AI4X initiative with two new postdoctoral fellows aimed at accelerating AI-driven research in materials science and engineering. The fellows will work on projects that use AI to speed up materials discovery, aligning with NTU's goal to integrate advanced technologies into its academic program.The announcement, made on 8th May 2026, positions NTU as a leader in applying AI to materials science and signals a broader shift toward technology-enabled research across disciplines.NTU's School of Materials Science and Engineering is a recognized leader; the addition of fellows is expected to reinforce its standing and further the interdisciplinary AI-materials collaboration.The move mirrors a growing trend in academia, where institutions recognize AI's role across fields. By embedding AI into materials research, NTU expands its research capacity and prepares students and faculty for technology-driven industries.As NTU continues to enhance its research initiatives, the appointment underscores NTU's commitment to innovation and excellence.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ NATIONAL UNIVERSITY OF SINGAPORE [3 News That Matters]

+ NUS UNVEILS MAGIC LAB AIMING TO SHAPE HUMAN-CENTRIC ROBOTIC INTELLIGENCE

The National University of Singapore's Computing department unveiled the MAGIC Lab, the Manipulation and General Intelligence Control Lab, on May 11, 2026, as a dedicated hub for advancing robotics AI through human-centric manipulation models. The lab will concentrate on research areas including robot learning, 3D vision, dexterous manipulation, and simulation, all geared toward real-world deployment and safe operation.This initiative places NUS Computing at the forefront of the shift toward embodied AI, where model reasoning must work in concert with sensor data, simulation, and deployment realities. The lab is actively seeking PhD students, research assistants, and postdoctoral researchers across robotics, computer vision, and machine learning to join interdisciplinary teams under the MAGIC umbrella.Duan, an incoming Presidential Young Professor, will lead the group and bring a track record that includes collaborations with Ai2, NVIDIA, and A*STAR’s Institute for Infocomm Research, along with leadership on MolmoAct, an open-source Robotics Foundation Model, illustrating his capacity to translate theory into practice.

+ SRMIST, NUS PARTNER TO ROLL OUT 15-MONTH PGDM-X FOR WORKING PROFESSIONALS

SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) and the National University of Singapore (NUS) have joined hands to offer the 15-month Post Graduate Diploma in Management for Executives (PGDM-X), a program designed for working professionals who want to accelerate leadership without leaving their careers. The program starts on 11th May 2026.Structured as a modular experience, the program features six on-campus residencies at SRMIST, five online inter-residency phases, and a seven-day international immersion at the NUS campus in Singapore, with intensive classroom learning, case-based discussions, leadership simulations, and industry interactions.Curriculum mixes foundational management disciplines with future-ready capabilities. Key modules include Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning for Business Analytics, Digital Transformation and India Stack, Strategic Marketing Simulation, Leadership Communication, Entrepreneurship and Startup Management, Organisational Leadership, Financial Markets, Supply Chain Management, Design Thinking and Project Management, among others.Participants will also have mentorship and interactions with global professionals, policy advisors, entrepreneurs and innovation leaders across sectors including AI, finance, sustainability, healthcare and technology.The international immersion is a highlight, with a week at the NUS campus featuring global insight courses, curated industry visits and executive masterclasses. The programme fee is Rs 12.8 lakh, covering tuition, the Singapore immersion, accommodation, academic materials and travel related to the Singapore component.The collaboration underscores the growing trend of integrating technology and global perspectives in executive education to prepare leaders for a globally interconnected business environment.

+ IPB UNIVERSITY ADVANCES INTERNATIONALIZATION WITH NUS COLLABORATION

IPB University is stepping up its internationalization drive through strategic alliances, with a core emphasis on collaboration with the National University of Singapore (NUS). FEM's double degree program expansion is already underway, with the partnership with NUS about 70 percent complete as the two institutions align curricula, credit recognition, and student mobility to raise the program’s global standing. Beyond NUS, IPB is growing its master’s double degree offerings to 15 programs and actively seeking additional partnerships with institutions in Malaysia, Turkey, and elsewhere to broaden opportunities for qualified students. The push is part of a broader plan to deliver globally standardized education that meets current labor-market demands and enhances IPB’s international profile. The university’s internationalization strategy includes launching international classes in 2024, which has seen IPB welcome a third cohort of students from diverse backgrounds, including Afghanistan. The International Thematic Real Work Lecture (KKNT) program, implemented with partners in Malaysia and Thailand, provides overseas learning experiences for about 50 students each year, reinforcing IPB’s commitment to experiential and cross-border education. In parallel with academic programs, IPB is investing in facilities that strengthen learning and research. An Investment Gallery in partnership with the Indonesia Stock Exchange is in development, and a Capital Market School for faculty and staff is being planned to boost investment literacy. Research output is rising as well, with the average publications per faculty member increasing from 0.4 to 0.8 over the past two years, signaling growing scholarly impact. Prof Irfan Syauqi Beik, Dean of FEM, stresses that the FEM should become a national and international reference in economics and management and play a role in shaping policy for national development.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HONG KONG UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY [3 News That Matters]

+ HKUST ADVANCES MEDICAL EDUCATION WITH GROUNDBREAKING CAMPUS PROJECT

The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology marked a milestone with the groundbreaking of its medical education and research complex on 29th April 2026, underscoring the university’s expanded push into healthcare education. Hong Kong’s Chief Executive John Lee attended the ceremony and reiterated government support, aligning the project with the national 15th Five-Year Plan to position Hong Kong as an international hub for high-calibre talent. The project carries a budget of HK$2 billion to fund an eight-story building scheduled for completion in mid-2028, with the first cohort of medical students expected to be admitted soon after. HKUST president Nancy Ip described the school’s breadth, including artificial intelligence, data science, healthcare management, medical ethics and health policy, and stressed the importance of collaboration with the city’s established medical schools. Ip noted that HKUST intends to build on existing achievements and contribute to Hong Kong’s evolution as a global center for medical innovation, and that the university was approved in November 2025 to establish the city’s third medical school, with plans for a four-year second-degree medical program to meet rising demand for healthcare professionals.

+ HKUST REVEALS TECH-DRIVEN MEDICAL SCHOOL EMPHASIZING AI, DATA SCIENCE

HKUST is advancing a landmark project to modernize medical education in Hong Kong, following last year's government decision to create the city's third medical school. A groundbreaking ceremony for the Medical Education and Research Complex took place on 28th April 2026.Chief Executive and HKUST Chancellor John Lee said the initiative would raise capacity and efficiency of the territory's healthcare services.The school is planned to admit its first 50 students in the 2028/29 academic year, with the Government providing matching funds and land reserved in the Northern Metropolis for a teaching hospital that will integrate medical education, research and patient care.HKUST President Nancy Ip spoke of a focus on technology and interdisciplinary collaboration, while HKUST Council Chairman Harry Shum highlighted alignment with the National 15th Five-Year Plan and the Healthy China strategy.The eight-storey complex near Shaw Auditorium will span about 6,000 square metres and house a clinical skills and simulation centre, small-group study rooms and teaching laboratories, with a new medical library planned to support students and faculty. The Dean search is progressing, with several clinical professors already appointed and interest from prospective faculty across regions.HKUST says the school will emphasize biotechnology, artificial intelligence and robotics in healthcare and will avoid poaching faculty from existing Hong Kong medical schools, preferring collaboration and cross-institutional electives while discussions with the Hospital Authority and Health Bureau continue on establishing the teaching hospital.

+ HKUST'S NEW MEDICAL CAMPUS SET TO ADDRESS DOCTOR SHORTAGE AND AGING POPULATION

Hong Kong can expect better healthcare as HKUST moves into the construction phase of its medical education and research complex, according to Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu at the groundbreaking this morning. The project marks the city's third medical school since government approval last November.Lee said the development will help attract and train top talent and contribute to Healthy China, a key goal in the Five-Year Plan. He added that the new medical school would improve quality, boost efficiency and capacity, and strengthen local doctor training to ease manpower shortages and cope with an aging population.Three working groups from government departments and experts will guide HKUST through curriculum planning and financing, he noted. He also indicated that land at Ngau Tam Mei in the Northern Metropolis has been reserved for the medical school's permanent campus and an integrated hospital for education, research, and patient care.HKUST president Nancy Ip Yuk-yu provided details: the eight-story complex will cover 6,000 square meters and is slated for completion by mid-2028. It will include a clinical training center and research laboratories, with seven clinical professors from the US, Europe, and Australia already hired, and a dean of medicine expected to be named soon. Ip stressed that HKUST will not poach from Hong Kong's two existing medical schools and highlighted potential collaboration, noting HKUST students may take electives at other schools and that there are ongoing discussions to broaden talent development.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO [3 News That Matters]

+ UTOKYO UNVEILS AUTONOMOUS LAB WITH 10 ROBOTS TO REDEFINE EXPERIMENTAL WORKFLOWS

The University of Tokyo has revealed a state-of-the-art robotics laboratory designed to run experiments previously performed by human researchers. The facility sits at the Institute of Science Tokyo's Yushima campus and officially opened on 12th May 2026, showcasing ten robots, including the Maholo LabDroid, operating without on-site staff. The Robotics Innovation Center intends to scale up its automated workforce and embed automation and AI across the research cycle. Current machines are equipped with dual arms capable of transferring precise reagent volumes and controlling temperature-sensitive equipment, with programming for automatic cell cultivation. Keiichi Nakayama, head of the center, says the project aims to lift Japan's scientific standing globally by treating AI and robotics as essential tools. The Maholo robot has already proven useful in Kobe, assisting in clinical research with induced pluripotent stem cells, and researchers from that hospital have joined the Tokyo center to widen collaboration. The university's long-range plan calls for expanding the robotic workforce to about 2,000 by 2040, with automatons taking on many tasks from hypothesis generation to experimental verification.

+ KDDI TEAMS WITH ALUMNI VENTURES AND UTOKYO TO ACCELERATE GLOBAL STARTUP GROWTH

KDDI has announced new partnerships with Alumni Ventures and UTokyo Innovation Platform to expand cross-border startup growth. The alliances aim to connect Japanese and international startups with funding, mentoring, and market access, reflecting a broader push by KDDI to position Japan more prominently in the global venture ecosystem.The arrangement leverages Alumni Ventures' U.S. network and UTokyo's Innovation Platform in Japan to tap into a steady flow of early-stage companies. KDDI intends to bring its network infrastructure, large customer base, and technology services to support pilots and scale opportunities, especially in 5G, IoT, and security-focused solutions.With Alumni Ventures in the United States and UTokyo Innovation Platform in Japan, KDDI seeks to widen its venture pipeline and access technologies that could complement its existing offerings. The partnerships are designed to help KDDI bolster its fintech ambitions while diversifying revenue streams beyond a saturated domestic mobile market.For investors, the collaboration signals a commitment to engaging with global startup flows at an earlier stage and could broaden opportunities for minority investments and partnerships.Executions and prioritization will matter: KDDI will need to show how it selects startups aligned with telecom and fintech objectives and whether management focus and capital are directed toward these programs without neglecting the core business. Key milestones the market will scrutinize include the launch of pilots on KDDI networks, the rollout of new digital consumer services, and enterprise offerings that integrate cybersecurity and IoT. Any disclosures of capital committed will shape confidence in the broader diversification strategy.

+ TOKYO AND CLAREMONT FORGE CROSS-BORDER MANGA JOURNALISM INITIATIVE

The University of Tokyo is expanding its curriculum by integrating manga under a new cross-cultural collaboration with The Student Life, aimed at fostering understanding between students in Tokyo and those in Claremont, California. The partnership was officially announced on 9th May 2026, signaling the university's commitment to innovative, culturally rich education across borders.Central to the partnership is the Todai Shimbun, the university's newspaper, which is leading the effort to promote cross-border cultural exchange and student journalism. The collaboration will allow students from both campuses to explore diverse writing styles and cultural narratives, deepening mutual understanding.At the heart of the program is the manga seminar taught by Associate Professor Kentaro Miwa from the Graduate School of Arts and Sciences. Since 2024, Miwa has introduced first-year students to the academic analysis of manga, guiding them through works from classic to contemporary and cultivating critical thinking.Students will choose a manga work and craft essays that analyze its themes and cultural significance. The assignments have spurred thoughtful discussion, with participants examining Osamu Tezuka's classics alongside newer titles such as 'Attack on Titan.' The university emphasizes interdisciplinary study, linking manga to broader visual culture.As the university strengthens international ties, the collaboration positions it as a leader in innovative educational practices that embrace global perspectives and cross-border learning.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TSINGHUA UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ MIGRASOMES AS LONG-RANGE SIGNALING PLATFORMS: TSINGHUA MAPS NEW CELLULAR DIALOGUE

Tsinghua University researchers have mapped migrasomes, organelles that resemble pomegranate-like structures and enable long-range communication between cells. As of 9th May 2026, the work adds a detailed perspective to a discovery first described about a decade ago.\n\nHistorically, intercellular signaling has centered on direct cell contact or soluble signals. Migrasomes address this gap by acting as platforms that package information for nearby or distant targets, proving resilient in dynamic tissue environments.\n\nSize and cargo: migrasomes measure about 0.5–3 m in diameter and carry chemokines, cytokines, growth factors, and other cargo such as mRNAs and damaged mitochondria, underscoring their broad role in cellular physiology.\n\nFormation sequence: researchers outline a three-stage process—nucleation, maturation, and expansion—that ensures migrasomes are produced at the right times and locations, enabling precise information delivery.\n\nImpact: migrasomes have guided embryonic development in zebrafish, promoted angiogenesis in chicken embryos, and contributed to vascular stability in adults, while their role in initiating rapid coagulation during injury highlights physiological balance. Ongoing work at Tsinghua may drive new diagnostics and therapies.

+ AI-POWERED MUST TELESCOPE SET TO MAP THE COSMOS IN COLLABORATION WITH EPFL

Tsinghua University is pursuing a groundbreaking venture with its Multiplexed Survey Telescope (MUST), a project that merges artificial intelligence with cutting-edge astronomical technology to reveal the nature of dark matter and dark energy. Scheduled for completion by 30th December 2030, MUST will capture light spectra from tens of millions of galaxies to construct a three-dimensional map of the cosmos.To manage the vast data stream, researchers have built AI algorithms tailored for high-dimensional cosmology. These foundation models, trained on extensive simulated universes, can identify delicate gravitational lensing effects and cosmic expansion signatures that are often buried in noise, enabling accelerated assessments compared with conventional methods.A key collaboration with EPFL will supply the MUST fiber positioner, expanding the project’s reach across the global astrophysics community. The blend of precision optics and advanced AI software positions Tsinghua at the vanguard of discovery, with the potential to chart dark matter haloes and quantify dark energy’s repulsive force. As one lead astrophysicist at Tsinghua said, 'MUST is not merely a telescope; it represents a highly reusable engine of discovery capable of bringing hidden physics to light.'As the project progresses, MUST could redefine how astrophysical research is conducted, extending the capabilities of engineering and data processing and opening new avenues for exploring the universe.

+ TSINGHUA ALUMNI-BACKED STARRING ENERGY NETS 500 MILLION RMB SERIES A+ ROUND

Starring Energy, a nuclear fusion venture originated at Tsinghua University, announced a 500 million RMB Series A+ on May 7, 2026, with participation from Fortune Capital, Jinpu Investment, and Shanghai Shenneng Chengyi, while Feitu Venture Capital continued its backing.The round raises total funding to over 2 billion RMB and elevates the company’s valuation above 1 billion USD, earning unicorn status.Founded in 2021 by Chen Rui and Tan Yi, the company pursues controlled fusion through its compact spherical tokamak technology.Its fundraising history includes a June 2022 angel round led by Sequoia China Seed Fund and Matrix Partners China, followed by a March 2024 Pre-A, reflecting growing investor confidence in its approach.Fortune Capital says controlled fusion is a cornerstone of future energy and energy independence, and Starring Energy’s work on high-temperature superconducting spherical tokamak systems places it at the forefront of private fusion.Looking ahead, the company is advancing multiple device generations, including SUNIST-2 already in operation and NTST under construction, with plans for its first engineering demonstration reactor.

+ UNIVERSITY OF HONG KONG [3 News That Matters]

+ JOCKEY CLUB ENDOWS HKU PROFESSORSHIPS, FUNDS GLOBAL SUSTAINABILITY INSTITUTE

A donation exceeding HK$426 million from The Hong Kong Jockey Club will fund HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute and establish The Hong Kong Jockey Club Endowed Professorships at The University of Hong Kong, with official dedication on 5th May 2026.The initiative aims to blend the insights of renowned international scholars with local needs, fostering a robust exchange of ideas to inform policy. Rimsky Yuen, Jockey Club Steward, noted that the endowed professorships will help translate research into actionable solutions for a more resilient and livable Hong Kong.The dedication ceremony was attended by senior figures from both groups, highlighting the collaboration's ambitions. The event underscored the goals of the joint effort to advance sustainability scholarship and practical impact.Endowed professors named so far include Professor Guojun He in Economics, Professor Jeffrey Ng in Accounting, and Professor Uta Schonberg in Economics, among others. The HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute aims to become a premier center for Environmental, Social, and Governance practices. Its core objectives include conducting impactful research, engaging with the community through sustainability indices and advisory services, and curating educational programs to prepare future professionals in sustainability.

+ HKJC FUNDS NEW ESG RESEARCH HUB AT HKU WITH OVER HK$426 MILLION

On Wednesday, 6th May 2026, the Hong Kong Jockey Club unveiled a donation of more than HK$426 million to the University of Hong Kong to establish the HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute. The funds will support research and operations and will also create the Hong Kong Jockey Club Endowed Professorships to attract leading international scholars whose work addresses local needs.A dedication ceremony for the initiative was held recently, underlining the goal of translating research findings into practical policy recommendations and solutions tailored to Hong Kong’s unique challenges.Rimsky Yuen, Steward of the HKJC, described the institute as a potential world-class hub for ESG best practices, with a focus on research, engagement and education, designed to generate insights with meaningful impact for Hong Kong and the wider region.

+ HKU LAUNCHES ECHECKGO AI TO SPEED UP URBAN BUILDING ASSESSMENTS

The University of Hong Kong has introduced eCheckGo, an artificial intelligence system aimed at transforming building inspections in crowded city environments. The iLab team, headed by Professors Junjie Chen and Wilson Lu of the Department of Real Estate and Construction, described the system on 29th of April 2026 as capable of rapid defect detection and thorough building assessments.As the stock of aging buildings grows, an estimated 14,000 privately owned structures are expected to be over 50 years old by 2030. HKU says eCheckGo can analyze thousands of images in seconds, delivering performance that is at least 100 times faster and eight times more cost-effective than conventional automated solutions, enabling proactive condition monitoring that supports public safety efforts.The platform uses a proprietary Large Defect Model, or LdM, trained on large datasets to ensure accurate defect detection across different building types. Users can snap photos with a mobile app or pull images from Google Street View, allowing for a seamless inspection workflow. Once uploaded, the AI flags defects such as cracks and spalling and creates interactive 3D models to visualize a building’s condition for maintenance planning.In a city-wide trial, the team used Google Street View imagery for 9,172 Kowloon buildings, generating a color-coded defect map in four hours. The findings were corroborated by professional building surveyors, underscoring the system’s accuracy and potential practical value.Professor Chen emphasized the importance of timely inspections and noted that eCheckGo offers an intuitive overview that can support quick responses to defects. The project has attracted interest from government agencies and industry partners, with ongoing discussions about its wider adoption, including expansion to water leakage detection and automated reporting.

+ KYOTO UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ REGIONAL SCHOOL AWA HIGH ACHIEVES FIRST UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO AND KYOTO UNIVERSITY ENROLLMENTS IN 33 YEARS

TATEYAMA, Chiba — Students from Chiba Prefectural Awa High School have achieved admissions to the University of Tokyo and Kyoto University this spring, a milestone announced on the 3rd of May 2026 and the first time in 33 years. The school, based in Tateyama, has battled enrollment quotas driven by depopulation and competition from tuition-free private schools that reduced the pool of applicants. Despite its 126-year history and the notoriety of Yoshiki as an alumnus, the campus had struggled to sustain academic prestige. A Renaissance Project, begun in 2023, established a specialized advanced class designed to prepare students for competitive national universities. Among the first graduates, Hyuga Shimabuku earned admission to UT's Faculty of Science Division II, while Hayato Takiguchi joined Kyoto University's Faculty of Agriculture.

+ TUJ KYOTO EXPANDS CAMPUS FOOTPRINT AS ENROLLMENT SURGES

TUJ Kyoto commemorates its first anniversary with notable growth, tripling enrollment since opening and adding new academic programs, greater classroom capacity, and upgraded facilities to meet demand. By 23rd April 2026, engagement has surged, underscored by the campus's inaugural TEDxTUJ event held earlier this month.The campus opened its second major site near the Fushimi Inari Shrine in January 2025 and has since expanded its footprint, adding a further classroom floor and increasing housing to more than 300 apartments, many within walking distance of the campus, reinforcing its commitment to a supportive, community-driven environment.The TEDxTUJ event, themed "Shoshin ( , Beginner’s mind)", highlighted the school’s global community through a slate of student-led presentations and discussions featuring TUJ staff, faculty, and students, reflecting the institution’s mission as a bridge across cultures. Looking ahead, the university plans to roll out an Honors Program and a Master of Science in Communication Management in September 2025, with an Adult Study Abroad program set to begin in May 2026.

+ 2 M BAND PCSEL BREAKTHROUGH BY AKM AND KYOTO UNIVERSITY PROMISES ENHANCED ENVIRONMENTAL SENSING

AKM and Kyoto University Institute for Advanced Study reported a laser-oscillation demonstration in the 2 m-band PCSEL achieved through joint development and presented at the 73rd JSAP Spring Meeting (March 15-18, 2026).\n\nPCSEL technology traces back to the early 2000s at Kyoto University led by Susumu Noda and is prized for delivering high directionality and narrow linewidth in a compact form factor, a key advantage over traditional lasers for compact sensing systems.\n\nThe 2 m window includes absorption lines of greenhouse gases such as CO2 and CH4, making this technology attractive for environmental monitoring. By refining the light-source structure, AKM and Kyoto University achieved the 2 m operation, enabling potential improvements in trace-gas measurements as well as healthcare-related sensing.\n\nAKM plans to accelerate R&D focusing on manufacturability and photonic-crystal design, aiming to position the company at the forefront of the evolving optoelectronics market where demand for precise infrared lasers is rising.

+ PEKING UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ PEKING UNIVERSITY, HSE STRENGTHEN SINO-RUSSIAN ACADEMIC COLLABORATION

A significant meeting in Moscow brought together HSE University's leadership and Peking University President Gong Qihuang to discuss strengthening ties under the Cross Years of Russian–Chinese Cooperation in Education for 2026-2027. On 5th May 2026, Anisimov and Gong Qihuang stressed the collaboration active since 2019 and cited six agreements signed in May 2025 with high-level participation. The parties discussed joint research in AI, ML and mathematics, with Peking University offering to facilitate academic exchanges through the Sino–Russian Association of Fundamental Sciences. Anisimov noted rising interest in Chinese studies at HSE, with nearly 1,500 students enrolled in Chinese language courses. Gong Qihuang emphasized youth engagement, expanding student mobility, potential double degree offerings, and collaborative dissertation training. He welcomed establishing a dedicated unit at HSE to oversee China projects. The delegation will join International Partners Week at HSE in Saint Petersburg from May 18-22, 2026, and Anisimov was invited to visit Peking University later this year for broader international forums.

+ ALUMNI-BACKED HARD-TECH IPOS SPOTLIGHT PKU'S MARKET IMPACT

Peking University turned 128 this year, but its influence extends beyond campus into a growing hard-tech IPO wave led by alumni, particularly since May 4, 2026. In 2025, Minglue Technology, Novosense Microelectronics, and Jingfang Pharmaceuticals went public, and this year Luoshi Robotics and Shenyan Intelligence have advanced their IPOs, signaling a broader trend. The university’s innovation ecosystem, supported by the Yuanpei Fund, Yanyuan Venture Capital, and the Yanyuan Incubator, creates a pipeline from laboratory breakthroughs to market success. Prominent examples include Galaxy Universal Robotics and Deep Potential Technology, which have drawn major funding and unicorn status; Galaxy Universal has raised over 6 billion yuan and sits at a valuation of roughly 21 billion yuan, while Deep Potential is applying AI to advance research tools. As PKU continues to cultivate its science and technology innovation ecosystem, the alumni network is poised to play an increasingly vital role in shaping the future of hard tech in China, turning Weiming Lake into a symbol of this transformation.

+ LINGNAN UNIVERSITY AND PEKING UNIVERSITY PARTNER FOR JOINT STEM SUMMER ACADEMY

Lingnan University and Peking University are coordinating the Lingnan-Yuanpei STEM Summer Academy (LYSSA) 2026, a programme designed to advance innovation and entrepreneurship among students. The opening ceremony took place virtually on Apr. 21, 2026. The seven-day programme will be hosted on Lingnan's Hong Kong campus from July 2-8 and will cover topics such as Intelligent Device and System for Real-Time Mosquito Monitoring, Darlink—an AI-powered communication platform—and SenseShield, which aims to improve accessibility to feminine hygiene products for people with disabilities.\n\nThe collaboration is positioned to align with the ongoing tech innovation surge and to help students translate ideas into viable business plans. Participants will be competing for incubation funds offered by the Lingnan Foundation, with Peking University having hosted LYSSA in the previous year as part of the joint effort.\n\nProf. Li Meng, Dean of Yuanpei College at Peking University, emphasized that the programme’s insights and networks will support students in realizing entrepreneurial visions. LEI director Prof. Albert Ko Wing-yin led the first training session on Making Impact Through Social Innovation and Humanitarian Technology and urged projects to deliver community benefits, echoing Lingnan's motto, Education for Service. The collaboration also aims to strengthen the Study in Hong Kong brand and develop high-calibre talent.

value.Title: Insight Global Invests in Renewable Energy Through New Solar Partnership

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-83F3-RT2K-70SX-00000-00Insight Global Invests in Renewable Energy Through New Solar Partnership2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisInsight Global Invests in Renewable Energy Through New Solar PartnershipRICHMOND, Va., May 13, 2026

PR NewswireThe company's first direct clean energy initiative marks its continued commitment to sustainability and community impact.RICHMOND, Va., May 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Insight Global, an international talent and consulting company, announced today it has made investments in renewable energy with the installation of a rooftop solar energy system in Richmond, Virginia. The project has been operational since early spring of this year and is Insight Global's first direct clean energy initiative in the company's overall climate strategy.

The installation includes a 112.89-kilowatt solar array made up of 210 panels, expected to generate approximately 156,647 kilowatt-hours of renewable electricity annually. The energy will be used to help power Insight Global's day-to-day operations at the Monument Consulting office, an Insight Global subsidiary located on Summit Avenue."We're making this investment because it's the right move for our business and the right move for the future," said Jenny Sabo, Senior Vice President of Communications, Benefits, & ESG. "Our partnership with Cherry Street Energy allows us to step into renewable energy in a way that's both operationally smart and financially sound."Insight Global operates primarily in leased office spaces, which traditionally can limit control over building infrastructure. Through a strong partnership with the building's owner, who is also the founder of Monument Consulting, the company was able to move forward with this meaningful investment."As IG increased our attention on sustainability and solutions, I realized I had a unique opportunity to support the company through my ownership of Monument Consulting's office building and the ability to evaluate solar energy generation from our rooftop," said Matt Aprahamian, Founder of Monument Consulting. "We are implementing a solution that will have a significant impact on our organization and our communities."This solar energy system was financed and installed in partnership with Atlanta-based power provider Cherry Street Energy, who entered into a 20-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with Insight Global."We welcome Insight Global into our distributed power network," said Michael Chanin, founder and CEO of Cherry Street Energy. "Whether it's schools, manufacturers, the World's Busiest Airport, or one of the largest talent and consulting companies in the U.S., our platform helps customers control energy market variability with reliable, affordable electricity."As Insight Global continues to advance its own sustainability initiatives, the company also partners with customers to deliver talent and consulting solutions that support their environmental goals. Examples of this consulting work include:Partnering with large, multi-site retailers to reduce waste and environmental impact tied to operational disruptions while protecting business continuity and controlling costs.Rethinking packaging for consumer technology companies by building dedicated packaging design engineering teams that deliver a premium customer experience while advancing sustainability objectives.Supporting a major telecom company's Regulated Waste Management (RWM) program to strengthen environmental compliance and reduce operational risk across installation yards and field operations. These efforts have successfully diverted thousands of regulated materials from improper disposal, helping keep harmful waste out of landfills and local ecosystems.About Insight Global:Insight Global is an international talent and consulting company that delivers business outcomes in an ever-changing world. We obsess over solving problems and building solutions that move our customers further, faster. With access to top talent in more than 50 countries, our tech-enabled recruiters can build teams quickly. Our technical experts across Cloud, AI, Data, Enterprise Operations, and Applied Engineering deliver solutions tailored to each customer's needs. As those needs evolve, so do we.As we evolve, though, we stay true to our purpose: to develop our people personally, professionally, and financially so they can be the light to the world around them. It shows up in everything we do, from investing in our people to delivering results for our customers to making a meaningful impact in our communities.   View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/insight-global-invests-in-renewable-energy-through-new-solar-partnership-302771365.htmlSOURCE Insight Global

value.Title: May 11, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJM-G8H3-S3TD-11S7-00000-00May 11, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 11, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. PEER LANDSCAPE: RECENT NEWS HIGHLIGHTS

On February 25, 2026, Consigli Construction Co. initiated work on the Williams College Museum of Art, marking the first purpose-built facility for the museum on campus. Abigail Berkebile, a key figure at Consigli, highlighted the project's complex and unique design, noting that it will significantly enhance student access to art collections. Berkebile also emphasized the wide array of opportunities available for women in the construction sector, pointing out that project teams rely heavily on expertise in accounting, IT, legal matters, marketing, procurement, quality control, and design. To ensure alignment on project goals during the build, Berkebile utilizes a structured approach of daily team connections and rigorous calendar management.

In recent developments for Suffolk Construction, the Virginia Department of Transportation announced the reopening of a long-term right lane closure on U.S. Route 58 westbound near downtown Suffolk, effective May 5, 2026. This lane reopening coincides with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction. The project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access the regional landfill without crossing westbound lanes. While the reopening allows three lanes of traffic to flow freely, temporary overnight closures from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m. will occur to remove temporary barriers, with a strict 50 miles per hour speed limit enforced by camera monitoring.

Additionally, on May 1, 2026, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement to source energy from Egg Power's 70-megawatt solar farm in Suffolk, which is currently under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement is set to supply approximately five percent of Virgin Media O2's total energy demands. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at Virgin Media O2, stated, "This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2's journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We're committed to growing and operating our business in a way that's good for people and the planet, where we're cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK."

For Turner Construction, the company achieved a significant milestone on April 28, 2026, by completing the structural phase of the 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland. The project logged over 80,000 craft hours with approximately 345 skilled workers. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company, noted, "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame. It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner's commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." The 450,000-square-foot facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution.

Also on April 28, 2026, Turner Construction commenced vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania. Steel is currently rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. David Kaminski, Vice President and Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, remarked, "This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come." The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028, supporting up to 3,800 construction jobs and generating approximately $1 billion in local economic impact.

In leadership news, Gilbane Development appointed Andreas Vlahakis as the head of capital markets on April 28, 2026. Operating from the New York City office, Vlahakis will refine and execute the firm's funding strategy, report directly to President and CEO James Patchett, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations. He brings experience from his previous role as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he led equity-raising efforts for various ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

Furthermore, on April 3, 2026, Gilbane Development began leasing Phase II of the Barnaby & 7th redevelopment in Southeast Washington, D.C. This phase includes 229 units across four new residential buildings slated for completion by the summer of 2026. Serving households earning 50 and 80 percent of the area median income, 20 percent of the apartments are designated as Permanent Supportive Housing units. The total project cost for Phase II was approximately $135 million. Phase I of the development, completed in the spring of 2025, included 169 apartments, and a third phase is planned to add roughly 100 additional rental units.

urn:contentItem:6JJ2-6M83-RX03-V10G-00000-00WEEKLY UPDATE: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 08, 20262026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:48ZLexisNexisWEEKLY UPDATE: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 08, 2026

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [2 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.
